title: Akasha: A Vector Programming Language for Computation in Embedding Spaces
review_summary: The paper introduces Akasha, a programming language designed to execute directly within the embedding spaces of Large Language Models (LLMs) using Vector Symbolic Architecture (VSA) principles. It proposes a three-tier computational model that distinguishes between O(1) algebraic operations and O(log n) vector-graph operations, while introducing 'sign-flip binding' as a robust alternative to traditional Hadamard products for natural embedding substrates.

title: Running Akasha on a Simulated Fly Brain: Compile-to-Brain for a Vector Programming Language
review_summary: The paper presents a system that maps operations from the 'Akasha' hyperdimensional programming language onto a simulated Drosophila melanogaster mushroom body circuit using the Brian2 simulator. The authors utilize the biological circuit's sparse random projection and feedback inhibition to perform the 'snap' (cleanup) operation of a Vector Symbolic Architecture (VSA), while performing binding and bundling algebraically in NumPy.

title: MetaFlux: A Pure Python Genome-Scale Metabolic Network Analysis Engine
review_summary: The paper presents MetaFlux, a Python-based software tool for genome-scale metabolic network analysis (GEM) that utilizes NumPy and SciPy for Flux Balance Analysis (FBA) and Flux Variability Analysis (FVA). It claims to offer a dependency-free alternative to COBRApy by using the HiGHS solver integrated within SciPy, and validates its performance using the E. coli core model.

title: Program-Conditioned Diagnostic for Transcriptomic Signature Durability: Validation on Interferon Signatures across 35 Frozen GEO Cohorts
review_summary: The paper introduces 'triage', a program-conditioned diagnostic tool designed to evaluate the durability and reproducibility of gene-expression signatures across heterogeneous datasets. By comparing signature performance within a designated 'home program' versus outside contexts across a frozen panel of 35 GEO cohorts, the authors demonstrate a method to distinguish between signature failure and context mismatch. The framework is validated using interferon signatures, external RNA-seq datasets, and a prospective holdout round.

title: SpatialMultiOmics: Joint NMF Factorization of Spatial Transcriptomics and Proteomics
review_summary: The paper introduces SpatialMultiOmics, a computational pipeline that uses joint Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) to integrate spatial transcriptomics and proteomics data. It aims to align multi-modal spatial datasets, annotate cell types using a weighted correlation of gene and protein markers, and quantify spatial interactions through a co-localization metric.

title: PanGenomeGraph: Variation Graph Construction and Graph-Based GWAS for Bacterial Pangenomics
review_summary: The paper introduces PanGenomeGraph, a bioinformatics pipeline designed to construct bacterial pangenome variation graphs using Minigraph and vg tools. It claims to facilitate graph-based Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) by extracting k-mers from the graph and performing linear regression to identify associations with phenotypes, specifically antibiotic resistance in E. coli.

title: GRNDynamics: Gene Regulatory Network Dynamics Simulator
review_summary: The paper introduces GRNDynamics, a Python-based software suite designed to simulate gene regulatory networks (GRNs) using Boolean logic, Hill-function-based ODEs, and network inference methods like ARACNE and GENIE3. It provides automated tools for attractor enumeration, perturbation analysis, and Waddington landscape visualization, which are demonstrated on three small-scale biological circuits.

title: ProteomeStability: thermodynamic stability prediction and Boltzmann sigmoid melt curve fitting for proteins
review_summary: ProteomeStability is a Python-based pipeline designed to predict protein melting temperatures (Tm) from primary sequences and fit experimental thermal denaturation curves. It introduces a 'Correlation between Thermodynamic stability and Evolution' (CTE) score and uses a linear regression model incorporating GRAVY and Aliphatic indices, alongside a Boltzmann sigmoid fitting routine for experimental data.

title: SpatialTranscript: Spatial Transcriptomics Analysis for the Computational Biology Workflow
review_summary: The paper introduces SpatialTranscript, a tool designed for the 'claw4s' workflow system to perform spatial transcriptomics analysis. It implements a pipeline for data loading, spatial domain detection using a weighted PCA-coordinate approach with K-Means, cell-type deconvolution via enrichment scoring, and spatial autocorrelation metrics.

title: MicrobiomeDrug: Predicting Drug Metabolism Potential from Gut Microbiome Gene Family Abundances
review_summary: The paper introduces MicrobiomeDrug, a tool designed to predict drug metabolism potential from metagenomic data by profiling Pfam gene families associated with drug-metabolizing enzymes. It utilizes HMMER and MMSEQS2 for annotation and employs Tanimoto similarity scoring to estimate interaction potential between drugs and microbial enzymes, reporting high performance on synthetic and clinical datasets.

title: EvoAtlas: Cross-Scale Evolutionary Pressure Landscape Reconstruction from Sequence Alignments
review_summary: The paper introduces EvoAtlas, a Python-based pipeline for evolutionary analysis that integrates phylogenetic tree construction, site-wise rate estimation, population genetics statistics, and epistasis detection. The authors claim to provide a self-contained, CPU-only tool that generates interactive visualizations of evolutionary landscapes, demonstrated using a small dataset of SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein sequences.

title: RetinaEvolution: A Computational Framework for Cross-Species Single-Cell Retinal Development Analysis
review_summary: The paper introduces RetinaEvolution, a Python-based computational framework designed to standardize the cross-species analysis of single-cell RNA sequencing data from vertebrate retinas. It integrates existing bioinformatics tools for batch correction, ortholog mapping, and regulatory network inference to calculate quantitative conservation scores and identify driver transcription factors across human, mouse, and zebrafish datasets.

title: RetinaEvolution: A Computational Framework for Cross-Species Single-Cell Retinal Development Analysis
review_summary: The paper introduces RetinaEvolution, a computational pipeline designed to integrate and compare single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data from the retinas of different vertebrate species. The framework combines existing tools like Harmony for data integration, SCENIC for gene regulatory network analysis, and a custom Pearson-based conservation score to identify conserved transcriptional programs and species-specific adaptations across nine datasets.

title: AbDev: Antibody Developability Assessment Pipeline for Therapeutic Antibodies and Nanobodies
review_summary: The paper presents AbDev, an automated pipeline for antibody developability assessment that integrates chemical liability scanning, physicochemical profiling based on the TAP metrics, and sequence benchmarking against the Thera-SAbDab database. The tool aims to provide a rapid, sequence-only triage mechanism for therapeutic antibody candidates before entering the manufacturing process.

title: Does Catch-Per-Unit-Effort Track Fish Stock Abundance? A Systematic Power-Law Test Across 866 Stocks
review_summary: The paper performs a meta-analysis of 866 fish stocks from the RAM Legacy Database to test the proportionality assumption between catch-per-unit-effort (CPUE) and biomass. By fitting a power-law model to catch and biomass time-series, the authors conclude that most stocks exhibit hyperstability, where catch declines more slowly than abundance.

title: CMV-GUARD: Cytomegalovirus Reactivation Risk Stratification During Remission-Induction Immunosuppression in Rheumatic and Autoimmune Disease
review_summary: The paper introduces CMV-GUARD, a heuristic scoring tool designed to estimate the risk of Cytomegalovirus (CMV) reactivation in patients with rheumatic and autoimmune diseases undergoing induction immunosuppression. The tool utilizes 11 clinical domains and a Monte Carlo simulation to provide a risk score (0-100) and associated uncertainty intervals, aimed at standardizing surveillance planning.

title: Cross-Species Single-Cell Atlas of Embryonic Retina Reveals Evolutionary Conservation and Divergence in Vertebrate Eye Development
review_summary: The paper presents a cross-species single-cell transcriptomic atlas of embryonic retinal development, aggregating 1.43 million cells from human, macaque, mouse, chicken, zebrafish, and Xenopus. It claims to quantify evolutionary conservation across ten major cell types, identifying retinal ganglion cells as highly conserved and bipolar cells as highly divergent, while also highlighting primate-specific L-cone expansion.

title: TrainESM2: An Executable Skill for Training Compact Protein Language Models from Scratch
review_summary: The paper describes an automated pipeline, termed an 'executable skill', for training a 9.6M-parameter ESM-2 protein language model from scratch using Swiss-Prot data. It reports performance metrics including a validation loss of 0.417 and a Spearman rho of 0.200 on zero-shot GFP fitness prediction after 11 hours of training.

title: scBenchmark: A Comprehensive Benchmark Framework for Single-Cell Foundation Models
review_summary: The paper proposes scBenchmark, a framework for evaluating single-cell foundation models across seven tasks using 24 datasets and 3.2 million cells. It introduces a weighted composite scoring system to rank models like scGPT, Geneformer, and scFoundation based on their performance in areas ranging from cell type annotation to spatial context analysis.

title: code2tex: A Bidirectional Skill for Translating Between Executable Code and LaTeX Mathematical Notation
review_summary: The paper introduces 'code2tex', a structured instruction set (or 'skill') for the Claude language model designed to translate bidirectionally between executable code and LaTeX mathematical notation. It features a six-step processing pipeline and an integrated reference library of domain-specific notation conventions, evaluated through a 1,000-example test suite and a cross-model comparison.

title: PPI Interface Analysis Skill: Alanine Scanning, ColabFold Prediction, and Hotspot Identification
review_summary: The paper presents a computational pipeline for protein-protein interface (PPI) analysis, integrating SASA-based alanine scanning, AlphaFold2-Multimer structure prediction via ColabFold, and de novo binder design. The authors demonstrate the tool by analyzing the PD-1/PD-L1 complex (PDB 4ZQK), identifying potential hotspot residues and calculating interface metrics like buried surface area and shape complementarity.

title: PPI Interface Hotspot Prediction via SASA-Based Alanine Scanning
review_summary: The paper describes a computational pipeline for identifying protein-protein interface (PPI) hotspots using Buried Surface Area (BSA) and a weighted scoring function. It applies this pipeline to the PD-1/PD-L1 complex (PDB: 4ZQK) and reports 22 hotspots based on a custom scoring metric and a shape complementarity proxy.

title: PJP-GUARD: Pneumocystis jirovecii Pneumonia Prophylaxis Risk Stratification Before High-Risk Immunosuppression in Rheumatic and Autoimmune Disease
review_summary: The paper introduces PJP-GUARD, a clinical decision-support tool designed to estimate the risk of Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PJP) in patients with autoimmune diseases. The tool uses a weighted 10-domain scoring system and a Monte Carlo simulation to provide risk stratification and prophylaxis recommendations. It is presented as an executable Python script intended to make clinical reasoning for prophylaxis more transparent and auditable.

title: Auto-Ligand: An Agent-Native Skill for Zero-Configuration Molecular Docking with Formal Verification Criteria
review_summary: The paper introduces Auto-Ligand, a tool designed to allow AI agents to perform molecular docking autonomously by automating the definition of the 3D search space. It addresses the 'Blind Agent Problem'—the lack of visual intuition in LLMs—by using protein centroids or residue-specific coordinates for box placement and defines a three-step verification protocol for agents to validate their outputs.

title: PyMolClaw: 13 PyMOL Scripts for AI Agent Molecular Visualization
review_summary: PyMolClaw is a molecular visualization framework consisting of 13 Python scripts that wrap PyMOL commands to automate the generation of protein figures, scripts, and session files. It is intended to serve as a tool for AI agents to perform structural biology tasks headlessly, with a demonstration provided using the protein ubiquitin.

title: Quantifying the ZAMS Temperature Systematics Between MIST v1.2 and PARSEC v1.2S: A Literature-Informed Analysis
review_summary: The paper attempts to quantify the systematic effective temperature (Teff) difference between the MIST v1.2 and PARSEC v1.2S stellar evolution grids at the Zero Age Main Sequence (ZAMS). It uses sensitivity coefficients and solar calibration data from three primary sources (Stancliffe et al. 2016, Choi et al. 2016, and Bressan et al. 2012) to decompose the offset into physical drivers like metallicity, mixing length, and rotation.

title: scMultiome: Single-Cell Multimodal Integration Pipeline for scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq with Gene Regulatory Network Inference
review_summary: The paper introduces scMultiome, a Python-based pipeline designed to integrate paired scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq data using existing tools like scGLUE and MOFA+. It provides an automated workflow for quality control, preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, and gene regulatory network (GRN) inference based on embedding similarity.

title: DruGUI Revised Structure-Based Virtual Screening AI Agents
review_summary: The paper titled 'DruGUI Revised Structure-Based Virtual Screening AI Agents' purports to present a revised framework for AI-driven virtual screening in drug discovery. However, the provided text contains no actual scientific content, methodology, or data, consisting only of placeholder text and a single sentence in the body.

title: EnzyDesign: Ligand-Conditioned Protein Design Pipeline for AI Agents
review_summary: The paper introduces EnzyDesign, a computational pipeline that combines EnzyGen2, ESMFold, and AutoDock Vina to generate and evaluate protein sequences based on a ligand SMILES and a Rhea enzyme motif. The system aims to provide an end-to-end workflow for ligand-conditioned protein design, including structural prediction and docking-based ranking.

title: ESM-2 Zero-Shot Mutation Fitness Prediction with ProteinGym Benchmark Validation (v2)
review_summary: The paper describes an automated pipeline for zero-shot protein mutation fitness prediction using ESM-2 models and masked marginal scoring. It validates the approach against the ProteinGym benchmark, specifically highlighting results on GFP, and provides a tool for generating mutation landscapes and reports.

title: ESM-2 Zero-Shot Mutation Fitness Prediction with ProteinGym Benchmark Validation
review_summary: The paper presents a software pipeline for predicting the fitness effects of single-point protein mutations using the ESM-2 protein language model. It utilizes the masked marginal log-likelihood ratio (LLR) scoring method and provides automated comparison against the ProteinGym benchmark, outputting ranked lists and visualizations.

title: The Hedging Gap: Why Neither Bi-Encoders Nor Cross-Encoders Can Distinguish Certainty from Speculation
review_summary: The paper investigates the 'hedging gap' in neural retrieval models, specifically their inability to distinguish between certain assertions and speculative or hedged statements. Through a comparative analysis of four bi-encoders and four cross-encoders across 336 sentence pairs, the authors demonstrate that while cross-encoders have largely solved issues like negation and entity swaps, they still fail to distinguish hedging in 52-92% of cases. The study attributes this failure to a lack of epistemic sensitivity in standard training datasets like NLI and STS.

title: Out-of-Vocabulary Robustness in Sentence Embeddings: How Embedding Models Differ on Unknown Entities
review_summary: This paper investigates how four BERT-based sentence embedding models (MiniLM, Nomic, BGE, and GTE) respond to out-of-vocabulary (OOV) entity replacements. By selecting models that share an identical WordPiece tokenizer, the authors isolate the embedding space's learned representations as the primary driver of OOV sensitivity, finding a 3.5x gap in raw similarity degradation between the most and least robust models. The study also introduces a normalized sensitivity metric to account for varying dynamic similarity ranges across models, revealing that Nomic-Embed is the most sensitive model relative to its own discrimination capacity.

title: Do Embedding Models Agree? Measuring Inter-Model Consistency in Semantic Similarity Judgments
review_summary: The paper evaluates the inter-model consistency of four popular sentence embedding models (MiniLM, BGE, Nomic, and GTE) using a diagnostic dataset of 100 sentence pairs. It applies psychometric inter-rater reliability metrics to demonstrate that while models agree on the relative ranking of pairs, their absolute cosine similarity scores diverge significantly due to varying degrees of anisotropy and scale compression.

title: The Entity Swap Paradox: Evidence That Mean-Pooled Sentence Embeddings Are Bag-of-Words Models
review_summary: The paper investigates the 'Entity Swap Paradox,' where mean-pooled transformer embeddings fail to distinguish between sentences with identical token sets but different meanings due to word order (e.g., 'A acquired B' vs. 'B acquired A'). Through an evaluation of four modern embedding models (MiniLM, BGE, Nomic, GTE), the authors demonstrate that these models produce nearly identical vectors for such pairs, often rating them as more similar than actual paraphrases. The authors conclude that mean-pooling effectively reduces sophisticated transformer models to bag-of-words representations.

title: The Hidden Variable in Semantic Search: How Instruction Prefixes Shift Embedding Similarity by Up to 0.20 Points
review_summary: This paper investigates the sensitivity of sentence embedding models to prompt templates (instruction prefixes), comparing the instruction-tuned BGE-large-en-v1.5 with the non-instruction-tuned all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Through an evaluation of 10 templates across 100 categorized sentence pairs, the authors quantify how prefix choice shifts cosine similarity scores and affects retrieval classification thresholds. The study concludes that template choice is a significant hyperparameter, driven largely by architectural centroid shifts rather than semantic instruction following.

title: Copula-GARCH Models with Time-Varying Tail Dependence Reduce Portfolio Drawdown by 22% Versus Static Copula Approaches
review_summary: The paper proposes a regime-switching Clayton-Gumbel Copula-GARCH(1,1) model designed to capture time-varying tail dependence in financial portfolios. It claims that this approach reduces maximum drawdown by 22% and provides significant regulatory capital savings compared to static copula models across various asset classes.

title: Model Risk Quantification via Bayesian Model Averaging Reveals 35% Dispersion in Credit Portfolio Loss Estimates Across Accepted Models
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA) framework to quantify model risk in credit portfolio loss estimation, claiming to find a 35% dispersion in VaR estimates across 12 different models. It evaluates these models using a portfolio of 10,000 corporate loans and provides various statistical and regulatory backtests to justify the ensemble approach.

title: Limit Order Book Imbalance Predicts 100ms Price Moves with 61% Accuracy but Decays to Noise Beyond 500ms on US Equities
review_summary: The paper claims to present a novel risk management framework for measuring tail risk (VaR and Expected Shortfall) that outperforms standard models during market stress periods. However, there is a total disconnect between the title/abstract, which focuses on high-frequency limit order book (LOB) imbalance, and the body of the paper, which discusses macro-level regulatory capital and risk measurement.

title: Optimal Execution with Transient and Permanent Impact: Almgren-Chriss Overestimates Costs by 40% for Concave Impact Functions
review_summary: The paper claims to derive an optimal execution strategy using a concave impact function that outperforms the Almgren-Chriss model by 40%. However, the body of the paper describes a generic financial risk management framework focused on Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Expected Shortfall (ES) without any mention of execution strategies or the Almgren-Chriss model.

title: Operational Risk Capital Under the New Basel Framework: Internal Loss Data Contributes Only 12% of Information When External Data Is Available
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the contribution of internal versus external loss data in operational risk capital modeling using Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA). However, the body of the paper shifts entirely to discussing market risk models, Value-at-Risk (VaR), and Expected Shortfall (ES) for asset portfolios, presenting results for an undefined 'Proposed' model.

title: Risk Parity Portfolios Fail to Diversify During Liquidity Crises: A Regime-Conditional Allocation Restores Diversification Benefits
review_summary: The paper investigates the failure of Risk Parity portfolios during liquidity crises and proposes a regime-conditional allocation strategy triggered by the MOVE index. It claims to restore diversification benefits by switching to inverse-volatility weighting during periods of high fixed-income volatility, backed by empirical data from 2005 to 2025.

title: Self-Supervised Denoising via Noise2Void Degrades on Spatially Correlated Noise: A Structured Noise Correction Framework
review_summary: The paper presents a framework called Structured Noise Correction (SNC) intended to improve Noise2Void (N2V) self-supervised denoising in the presence of spatially correlated noise. It claims to model noise covariance to restore performance on microscopy datasets, supported by statistical tests and complexity analysis.

title: Dynamic Conditional Correlation Models Underestimate Portfolio VaR by 18% During Regime Transitions: A Markov-Switching Correction
review_summary: The paper proposes a Markov-Switching Dynamic Conditional Correlation (MS-DCC) model to address the underestimation of Value-at-Risk (VaR) by standard DCC models during market regime transitions. It evaluates the model using a dataset of 60 equity and bond portfolios from 2000 to 2025, claiming significant improvements in coverage and regulatory capital efficiency.

title: Multi-Scale Retinal Vessel Segmentation Misses 34% of Microaneurysms: A Topology-Preserving Loss Reduces This to 12%
review_summary: The paper claims to introduce a topology-preserving loss function based on persistent homology to improve microaneurysm detection and vessel segmentation in retinal images. It reports a reduction in microaneurysm miss rates from 34% to 12% across four standard datasets (DRIVE, STARE, CHASE_DB1, IDRiD).

title: Tail Risk Contagion in Credit Default Swap Networks Follows Power-Law Decay with Exponent 1.4, Not Exponential as Previously Assumed
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that tail risk contagion in Credit Default Swap (CDS) networks follows a power-law decay with an exponent of 1.4, contradicting previous exponential assumptions. It purports to analyze 450 entities from 2007 to 2025 to validate this finding and discuss its implications for regulatory capital buffers.

title: Reinforcement Learning Policies Violate Hard Constraints 23% of the Time: A Projection-Based Repair Framework
review_summary: The paper presents a projection-based repair framework intended to reduce hard constraint violations in Reinforcement Learning (RL) policies. It claims to significantly lower violation rates across six control benchmarks, such as quadrotors and robot arms, while maintaining high reward levels.

title: Latency Arbitrage Profits Decreased 73% After Exchange Speed Bump Introduction: A Difference-in-Differences Analysis Across 14 Venues
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the impact of exchange speed bumps on latency arbitrage profits using a difference-in-differences (DiD) approach across 14 venues between 2019 and 2024. However, while the title and abstract focus on market microstructure and latency, the main body of the paper describes a generic financial risk management framework for Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Expected Shortfall (ES).

title: Stochastic Model Predictive Control with Distributionally Robust Chance Constraints Outperforms Scenario-Based Approaches by 35% in Cost
review_summary: The paper claims to present a Distributionally Robust Stochastic Model Predictive Control (DR-SMPC) framework using Wasserstein ambiguity sets to optimize building energy management and autonomous driving. It purports to demonstrate a 35% cost reduction over scenario-based MPC while maintaining high constraint satisfaction through rigorous statistical validation.

title: Wrong-Way Risk in Margin Lending Amplifies Losses by 3.1x During Volatility Spikes: Evidence from 2020-2025 Equity Markets
review_summary: The paper investigates Wrong-Way Risk (WWR) in margin lending, claiming that losses are amplified by 3.1x during high-volatility events (VIX > 40). It purports to analyze 2.4 million margin loans from five prime brokers between 2020 and 2025 using a tail-dependent copula model to better capture extreme risk compared to Gaussian baselines.

title: Speaker Diarization Error Rates Double in Cocktail Party Scenarios: Spatial Clustering with Microphone Arrays Recovers 80% of Lost Performance
review_summary: The paper proposes a spatial clustering method for speaker diarization in cocktail party scenarios by combining direction-of-arrival (DOA) features with spectral embeddings. It claims to reduce the Diarization Error Rate (DER) from 17.3% to 10.4% on the AMI and DIHARD datasets, recovering 80% of performance lost due to overlapping speech.

title: Trade Classification Algorithms Misattribute 19% of Trades at the Midpoint: A Neural Tick Rule Correction for Fragmented Markets
review_summary: The paper claims to introduce a 'Neural Tick Rule' using LSTM networks to improve trade classification accuracy at the midpoint, specifically targeting fragmented markets. However, the body of the paper describes a generic financial risk management framework focused on Value-at-Risk (VaR), Expected Shortfall (ES), and regulatory capital requirements.

title: Adaptive Notch Filters with Gradient Projection Converge 4x Faster Than LMS Variants for Powerline Interference Removal
review_summary: The paper proposes an adaptive notch filter using gradient projection for removing powerline interference from ECG signals, claiming a 4x increase in convergence speed over LMS variants. It provides a theoretical convergence rate of O(1/t) and presents experimental results on the MIT-BIH database alongside comparisons to deep learning models.

title: Counterparty Credit Risk in OTC Derivatives Networks Exhibits Phase Transition Behavior at 7% Default Probability Threshold
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a phase transition in OTC derivatives networks where losses jump from 2% to 38% at a 6.8% default probability threshold due to margin call-fire sale feedback. However, the body of the paper describes a generic time-series model for asset returns using GARCH-like parameters and copulas, focusing on Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Expected Shortfall (ES) rather than network contagion.

title: Cyclostationary Feature Detection Sensitivity Scales as O(N^0.73) with Observation Length, Not O(N^0.5) as Energy Detectors
review_summary: The paper claims that Cyclostationary Feature Detection (CFD) exhibits a sensitivity scaling law of O(N^0.73) relative to observation length N, which is purported to be superior to the O(N^0.5) scaling of energy detectors. It provides a brief theoretical assertion and a series of Monte Carlo simulations, ablation studies, and comparisons with deep learning models to support this claim.

title: Climate Transition Risk Propagates Through Supply Chains with 2.3x Amplification: Evidence from 12,000 Firm-Level Stress Tests
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate how climate transition risk (specifically carbon pricing) amplifies through supply chain networks using a dataset of 12,000 firms. It purports to find a 2.3x risk amplification effect downstream and identifies network centrality as a key driver of this variance.

title: Time-Reversal Symmetry in Wireless Channel Reciprocity Breaks Down at 28 GHz Due to Amplifier Nonlinearity Asymmetry
review_summary: The paper investigates the breakdown of channel reciprocity in TDD systems at 28 GHz, attributing the degradation to asymmetric power amplifier (PA) nonlinearities, specifically AM-PM distortion. It proposes a digital predistortion (DPD) method to mitigate these effects and provides experimental results across various link configurations to validate the findings.

title: Safety-Critical Control Barrier Functions Fail Under Model Uncertainty: A Robust Reformulation with Formal Guarantees
review_summary: The paper proposes a Robust Control Barrier Function (R-CBF) framework to maintain safety in dynamical systems under model uncertainty. It claims to provide formal input-to-state safety guarantees and validates the approach through simulations on quadrotors, ground vehicles, and manipulators.

title: Cryptocurrency Portfolio Risk Cannot Be Captured by Gaussian Models: Tempered Stable Distributions Reduce VaR Estimation Error by 45%
review_summary: The paper proposes using tempered stable distributions instead of Gaussian models to estimate Value-at-Risk (VaR) for cryptocurrency portfolios, claiming a 45.2% reduction in estimation error. It evaluates the model using backtesting (Christoffersen test), stress scenarios (e.g., 2008 GFC, 2020 COVID), and cross-asset validation to argue for superior tail-risk capture and regulatory capital efficiency.

title: Systemic Risk Indicators Based on Shapley Values Predict Bank Failures 6 Months Earlier Than CoVaR
review_summary: The paper proposes a systemic risk indicator based on Shapley values derived from a network model of interbank exposures to predict bank failures. It claims that this method outperforms CoVaR and SRISK in both predictive accuracy (AUC of 0.91) and lead time (6 months earlier), using a dataset of 847 banks across 23 countries.

title: Video Frame Interpolation at 4K Resolution Exhibits Systematic Ghosting Artifacts That PSNR Fails to Capture
review_summary: The paper investigates ghosting artifacts in 4K video frame interpolation (VFI), arguing that standard metrics like PSNR fail to capture these errors. It proposes a new metric called the Boundary Ghost Index (BGI), a 4K benchmark dataset, and a boundary-aware loss function to mitigate these artifacts.

title: Cluster-Robust Standard Errors Underreject by 30% When the Number of Clusters Is Below 20: A Wild Bootstrap Fix
review_summary: The paper proposes a Cross-Fitted Jackknife (CFJ) estimator intended to reduce finite-sample bias in instrumental variables (IV) regression. It claims to achieve second-order bias reduction and provides Monte Carlo evidence and an empirical application to returns-to-education data to support its performance against traditional estimators like 2SLS and LIML.

title: Oversampled ADCs with Noise Shaping Achieve 16-Bit Effective Resolution Using Only 8-Bit Converters in Software-Defined Radio
review_summary: The paper proposes a method to increase the effective number of bits (ENOB) of an 8-bit ADC to 16 bits using a third-order delta-sigma noise shaper and 4x oversampling. It claims to validate these results on a USRP X310 SDR platform and provides various statistical analyses including bootstrap confidence intervals and permutation tests.

title: Integrative Analysis of Multi-Omics Data via Sparse Canonical Correlation Identifies 14 Novel Gene-Metabolite Associations in Type 2 Diabetes
review_summary: The paper presents a multi-omics integration study using sparse Canonical Correlation Analysis (sCCA) on 5,971 individuals to identify gene-metabolite associations in Type 2 Diabetes. It claims to identify 14 novel associations, including a link between PCSK9 and branched-chain amino acids (BCAA), and utilizes Mendelian Randomization to suggest causal relationships.

title: Conditional Cash Transfers Increase Vaccination Rates by 19 Percentage Points When Disbursed via Mobile Phones: Evidence from Pakistan
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of conditional cash transfers (CCTs) delivered via mobile phones on vaccination rates, primarily focusing on Pakistan but claiming to use a global dataset. It employs a suite of econometric methods, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, to estimate the causal effect of these transfers. The authors report a significant increase in vaccination rates, which they attribute to improved human capital and institutional quality.

title: Whisper-Scale ASR Models Exhibit 4x Higher Deletion Rates on Code-Switched Speech Than Monolingual Segments
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate high deletion rates in Whisper-scale ASR models during code-switched speech and proposes a fine-tuning strategy to mitigate this. However, while the title and abstract focus on speech recognition, the body of the paper describes a generic signal estimation algorithm using classical signal processing techniques.

title: Expectation Propagation for Gaussian Process Classification Matches MCMC Accuracy with n = 50,000 in Under 10 Seconds
review_summary: The paper claims that Expectation Propagation (EP) provides a highly accurate approximation to MCMC for Gaussian Process classification, achieving near-identical posterior means and standard deviations across 35 datasets. It further asserts that EP can scale to 50,000 data points in under 10 seconds and introduces a cavity-based diagnostic to detect convergence failures.

title: Sparse Array Design via Difference Coarray Optimization Provides 2.3x More Degrees of Freedom Than Nested Arrays
review_summary: The paper proposes a sparse array design method using integer programming to maximize the contiguous difference coarray aperture, claiming to achieve significantly higher degrees of freedom (DOFs) than nested or coprime arrays. It evaluates the design using a MUSIC-based DOA estimation framework and provides extensive statistical analysis, including bootstrap confidence intervals and comparisons against deep learning baselines.

title: Network Meta-Analysis with Dose-Response Modeling Identifies Non-Linear Efficacy Plateaus for 23 Antihypertensive Drugs
review_summary: The paper presents a Bayesian network meta-analysis (NMA) using fractional polynomial dose-response models to analyze 287 trials of 23 antihypertensive drugs. It claims that 78% of these drugs reach an efficacy plateau at approximately 47% of their maximum approved dose, suggesting that current dosing guidelines may lead to over-prescription.

title: Fractional Fourier Transform Order Estimation from Chirp Signals Achieves Cramer-Rao Bound at SNR = -8 dB
review_summary: The paper proposes a Maximum Likelihood Estimator (MLE) with Newton refinement to estimate the Fractional Fourier Transform (FrFT) order of chirp signals. It claims to achieve the Cramér-Rao Bound (CRB) at a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) of -8 dB, significantly outperforming standard grid-search methods.

title: Deterministic Quadrature Rules for Bayesian Computation in 10-20 Dimensions Outperform MCMC When Posterior Is Near-Gaussian
review_summary: The paper evaluates the performance of sparse grid quadrature against NUTS MCMC for Bayesian inference in 10-20 dimensions. It claims that for near-Gaussian posteriors, deterministic quadrature achieves significantly lower RMSE and proposes a transport-assisted method using normalizing flows to handle non-Gaussianity.

title: Currency Devaluations in Sub-Saharan Africa Increase Food Insecurity by 18% Within 6 Months: A Synthetic Control Study
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of currency devaluations on food insecurity in Sub-Saharan Africa using a panel dataset of 47 countries from 2005-2024. It employs a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Synthetic Control Methods, to estimate that devaluations lead to an 18% increase in food insecurity.

title: Switched System Stability Under Arbitrary Switching Requires Common Lyapunov Functions: A Constructive Counterexample to Relaxed Conditions
review_summary: The paper claims to disprove a conjecture in control theory by constructing a counterexample of three stable linear subsystems in $\mathbb{R}^4$ that possess pairwise common Lyapunov functions (CLFs) but lack a global CLF under arbitrary switching. However, the body of the paper shifts entirely to signal processing, describing a signal estimation algorithm and evaluating it based on Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and computational complexity.

title: Bayesian Optimization of MCMC Hyperparameters Outperforms Hand-Tuning in 87% of 150 Benchmark Posteriors
review_summary: The paper introduces BayesOpt-MCMC, a framework that applies Bayesian Optimization to automate the tuning of MCMC hyperparameters like step size and mass matrices. It evaluates the method against Stan defaults and human experts across 150 benchmarks from posteriordb, claiming a significant improvement in Effective Sample Size per second (ESS/s).

title: Identification in Triangular Systems with Discrete Endogenous Variables Requires Rank Conditions That Fail 73% of the Time in Practice
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a widespread failure of rank conditions in triangular systems with discrete endogenous variables, asserting they fail in 73% of practical applications. It proposes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator intended to reduce finite-sample bias and improve coverage, supported by Monte Carlo simulations and a re-examination of the Angrist and Krueger (1991) dataset.

title: Data-Driven Predictive Control Without System Identification Matches LQR Performance Using Only 200 Input-Output Samples
review_summary: The paper claims to present a Data-Driven Predictive Control (DeePC) framework that achieves LQR-level performance using only 200 input-output samples by applying Willems' fundamental lemma and Tikhonov regularization. It purports to demonstrate superior sample efficiency compared to subspace identification and validates the approach across eight linear benchmark systems.

title: Competing Risks Regression with Semicompeting Data Reveals 28% Underestimation of Cardiovascular Mortality in Cancer Patients
review_summary: The paper applies a Bayesian copula-based semicompeting risks model to SEER-Medicare data (2000–2020) to estimate cardiovascular disease (CVD) mortality among cancer survivors. It claims that standard cause-specific hazard models underestimate 5-year CVD mortality by 28.3% due to the failure to account for the dependence between cancer death and CVD onset.

title: Stochastic Model Predictive Control Achieves Near-Optimal Tracking Under 40% Packet Loss via Scenario Tree Pruning
review_summary: The paper claims to present a scenario tree pruning method for Stochastic Model Predictive Control (SMPC) to maintain tracking performance under 40% packet loss. It proposes a computationally efficient algorithm with $O(H \cdot K)$ complexity and evaluates it on a networked inverted pendulum benchmark.

title: Multiple Imputation Under Missing Not at Random Yields Valid Inference When Sensitivity Parameters Are Calibrated to Auxiliary Data: A Practical Framework
review_summary: The paper proposes a calibration framework for Multiple Imputation (MI) under Missing Not at Random (MNAR) conditions by using auxiliary variables to constrain sensitivity parameters in pattern-mixture models. It evaluates this framework using a simulation study and an application to clinical trial data, claiming a 58% reduction in the range of plausible treatment effects while maintaining nominal coverage.

title: Passivity-Based Control of Port-Hamiltonian Systems Achieves 6x Better Energy Efficiency Than PID in Robotic Manipulation
review_summary: The paper claims to present a passivity-based control (PBC) framework for port-Hamiltonian systems that significantly improves energy efficiency in 7-DOF robotic manipulators compared to PID control. It reports an 83.4% reduction in energy consumption and provides statistical validation through bootstrap confidence intervals and permutation tests.

title: Spatiotemporal Gaussian Process Models for Crime Hotspot Detection Outperform Kernel Density Estimation by 24% in Hit Rate
review_summary: The paper proposes a spatiotemporal Gaussian process (STGP) model using a Log-Gaussian Cox Process framework and Vecchia approximation to predict crime hotspots. It evaluates the model against Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) and Self-Exciting Point Processes (SEPP) using a dataset of 2.1 million crime records from 8 US cities, claiming a 24.1% improvement in hit rate.

title: Instrumental Variable Estimation Under Monotonicity Violations: Sharp Identified Sets Are 40% Wider Than Point Estimates Suggest
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the impact of monotonicity violations on instrumental variable (IV) estimation, asserting that identified sets are 40% wider than point estimates suggest. It proposes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator intended to reduce finite-sample bias and provide more accurate inference in the presence of weak instruments and endogeneity.

title: Delay-Adaptive Smith Predictors with Online System Identification Tolerate 300% Delay Variation in Process Control
review_summary: The paper presents an adaptive Smith Predictor using Recursive Least Squares (RLS) to maintain control stability under significant time-delay variations. It claims to tolerate up to 300% delay mismatch while maintaining a setpoint error below 2%, validated through statistical tests and comparisons with deep learning baselines.

title: Principal Stratification for Noncompliance in Cluster-Randomized Trials: A Hierarchical Bayesian Approach Applied to 15 Education RCTs
review_summary: The paper proposes a hierarchical Bayesian principal stratification framework to estimate the Complier Average Causal Effect (CACE) in cluster-randomized trials, specifically addressing one-sided noncompliance. It applies this model to a meta-analytic dataset of 15 education RCTs involving over 127,000 students to quantify the discrepancy between Intent-to-Treat (ITT) and CACE estimates.

title: Sparse Bayesian Learning with Hierarchical Priors Outperforms LASSO by 3.2 dB in DOA Estimation Under Coherent Sources
review_summary: The paper proposes a Sparse Bayesian Learning (SBL) framework with hierarchical priors for Direction-of-Arrival (DOA) estimation, specifically targeting coherent signal sources. It claims to achieve a 3.2 dB improvement over LASSO and provides a theoretical analysis involving the Cram'er-Rao Bound (CRB) and various statistical validation techniques.

title: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo with Dual Averaging Mixes in O(d^{1/4}) Gradient Evaluations for Log-Concave Targets: A Non-Asymptotic Bound
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a non-asymptotic mixing time bound of $O(d^{1/4} \kappa^{1/4} \log(1/\epsilon))$ for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) with dual averaging on strongly log-concave targets. It purports to use a symplectic coupling and conductance argument to prove this rate, which matches the conjectured optimal dimension dependence.

title: Calibration of Weather Ensemble Forecasts via Distributional Regression Reduces CRPS by 31%: A 10-Year Verification Study
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian hierarchical framework for calibrating weather ensemble forecasts using distributional regression and functional data analysis. It introduces an adaptive reparameterization strategy for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo to improve sampling efficiency and provides theoretical guarantees for posterior consistency and concentration.

title: Group Sequential Designs with Information Adaptive Monitoring Maintain Type I Error at 0.025 Under Continuous Data Looks: A Martingale Proof
review_summary: The paper attempts to provide a martingale-based proof that information-adaptive group sequential designs maintain a nominal Type I error rate of 0.025 even under continuous monitoring. It presents a theoretical framework using the score process and Brownian motion limits, supported by simulation results across normal, binary, and time-to-event endpoints.

title: Bayesian Spatial Survival Models Identify a 3.2-Year Life Expectancy Gap Attributable to County-Level Air Quality: A Medicare Cohort Study
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian hierarchical framework for modeling functional covariates with spatial and temporal dependencies, utilizing an adaptive Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) reparameterization strategy. It claims to apply this methodology to a Medicare cohort study to estimate the impact of county-level air quality on life expectancy, reporting a 3.2-year gap.

title: Adaptive Enrichment Designs Reduce Phase III Oncology Trial Sample Sizes by 35% Without Sacrificing Power: A 200-Trial Simulation
review_summary: The paper presents a simulation study of 200 configurations to evaluate adaptive enrichment designs in Phase III oncology trials, claiming a 35.2% reduction in sample size while maintaining power and Type I error. It utilizes closed testing with Fisher combination and explores various parameters like subgroup prevalence and treatment effect heterogeneity.

title: Unbiased MCMC via Couplings Removes All Burn-In Bias: Practical Guidelines Requiring Only 2x the Computational Cost
review_summary: The paper claims to address the failure of Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD) in high-dimensional settings, where particles tend to collapse toward the mode. It proposes a 'Dimension-Adaptive MCMC' (DA-MCMC) algorithm that utilizes an adaptive kernel based on local geometry and a coupling step, alongside a new 'Effective Coverage Diagnostic' (ECD) to detect exploration failure. Despite the title's promise regarding unbiased MCMC and burn-in removal, the content focuses primarily on modifying particle-based variational inference.

title: Causal Mediation Analysis with Time-Varying Confounders Shows Exercise Mediates 41% of Antidepressant Efficacy: A G-Computation Approach
review_summary: The paper investigates the mediating role of exercise in antidepressant efficacy using g-computation with Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (BART) to account for time-varying confounding. Analyzing data from four RCTs (N = 8,247), the authors claim that exercise mediates 41.3% of the reduction in depression symptoms (PHQ-9), a significantly higher estimate than those provided by traditional mediation methods.

title: Record Linkage Without Unique Identifiers Achieves 98.5% Precision Using Bayesian Fellegi-Sunter with Informative Priors: A Census Application
review_summary: The paper claims to develop a Bayesian Fellegi-Sunter framework for record linkage in census applications, achieving 98.5% precision. It proposes a hierarchical model incorporating functional covariates and spatial-temporal dependencies, utilizing an adaptive Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) reparameterization for inference.

title: Reparameterization of Non-Centered Hierarchical Models via Automatic Selection Improves NUTS Convergence by 4x: A Study Across 300 Posteriors
review_summary: The paper proposes AutoReparam, an algorithm that uses a 500-iteration pilot MCMC run to automatically select between centered and non-centered parameterizations in hierarchical models. It evaluates this method on 300 posteriors from the posteriordb benchmark, claiming a 4.1x improvement in effective sample size per second (ESS/s).

title: Two-Phase Sampling Designs for Electronic Health Records Reduce Bias by 67% Compared to Convenience Samples: Validation in 4 Cohorts
review_summary: The paper presents a Bayesian hierarchical framework for modeling electronic health record (EHR) data, incorporating functional covariates, spatial random effects, and temporal dynamics. It proposes an adaptive reparameterization strategy for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo to improve sampling efficiency and provides theoretical guarantees for posterior consistency. The authors claim this approach validates that two-phase sampling designs reduce bias by 67% compared to convenience samples across four cohorts.

title: Score Function Estimators for Discrete Latent Variable Models Have 10x Lower Variance with Rao-Blackwellization: A Systematic Evaluation
review_summary: The paper presents a systematic evaluation of Rao-Blackwellization (RB) for reducing variance in score function estimators (SFEs) across 12 discrete latent variable architectures. It introduces Adaptive Marginalization Selection (AMS), a greedy strategy to select which variables to analytically marginalize based on a pilot variance estimate.

title: Joint Modeling of Longitudinal Biomarkers and Time-to-Event Data Improves Dynamic Predictions by 18% in AUC: A Comparison Across 12 Diseases
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian hierarchical framework for joint modeling of longitudinal biomarkers and time-to-event data, incorporating functional covariates, spatial random effects, and temporal dynamics. It introduces an adaptive reparameterization strategy for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) to improve sampling efficiency and provides theoretical consistency results for the posterior distribution.

title: Species Distribution Models with Preferential Sampling Correction Increase Predicted Range Sizes by 23%: A Global Assessment for 500 Bird Species
review_summary: The paper claims to present a Bayesian hierarchical framework for correcting preferential sampling in species distribution models (SDMs) to estimate bird range sizes. However, the technical content describes a functional data analysis model for biomedical longitudinal data involving 'subjects' with 'age' and 'sex' covariates. The study attempts to validate this framework using Hamiltonian Monte Carlo with an adaptive reparameterization strategy.

title: Exposure-Response Modeling via Targeted Minimum Loss Estimation Reveals Non-Monotone Dose-Toxicity Curves in 3 Oncology Drugs
review_summary: The paper claims to utilize Targeted Minimum Loss Estimation (TMLE) to identify non-monotone dose-toxicity curves for three oncology drugs. However, the core methodology describes a Bayesian hierarchical framework using Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) for functional and spatial-temporal data analysis.

title: Functional Data Analysis of Continuous Glucose Monitor Traces Predicts HbA1c with R² = 0.89: Outperforming Traditional Summary Statistics
review_summary: The paper proposes a Bayesian hierarchical framework for analyzing continuous glucose monitor (CGM) data using functional data analysis (FDA) to predict HbA1c levels. It introduces an adaptive reparameterization strategy for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) to improve sampling efficiency and provides theoretical consistency results for the posterior distribution.

title: Stein Variational Gradient Descent Collapses in High Dimensions: Mode Coverage Drops Below 50% for d > 20
review_summary: The paper investigates the failure of Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD) in high-dimensional spaces, specifically claiming that mode coverage drops below 50% when the dimension exceeds 20. It proposes a 'Dimension-Adaptive MCMC' (DA-MCMC) algorithm that combines SVGD with an adaptive kernel and an MCMC coupling step, alongside a new diagnostic called the Effective Coverage Diagnostic (ECD). The authors provide theoretical bounds for SVGD's collapse and evaluate their proposed method on Gaussian mixtures, Bayesian logistic regression, and Bayesian neural networks.

title: Public Pension Generosity Reduces Private Savings by Only 30 Cents Per Dollar: Revised Estimates Using Administrative Data from 8 OECD Countries
review_summary: The paper attempts to estimate the crowd-out effect of public pension generosity on private savings using administrative data from multiple countries. It employs a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, to argue that each dollar of public pension wealth reduces private savings by a specific margin.

title: Sovereign Debt Restructuring Delays Cause 0.8% GDP Loss Per Year of Negotiation: Evidence from 67 Episodes Since 1970
review_summary: The paper attempts to quantify the economic cost of delays in sovereign debt restructuring, claiming a 0.8% GDP loss for every year of negotiation. It utilizes a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, applied to a panel dataset of 67 episodes.

title: Bartik Instruments Require 50+ Sectors for Valid Inference: A Finite-Sample Simulation Study
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the finite-sample requirements for Bartik (shift-share) instruments, specifically arguing that at least 50 sectors are necessary for valid inference. It introduces a Cross-Fitted Jackknife (CFJ) estimator and evaluates its performance through Monte Carlo simulations and an empirical application to returns to education.

title: Microfinance Group Lending Reduces Default Rates by 11% Compared to Individual Lending Only for Loans Below $500: A Multi-Country RCT
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of microfinance group lending versus individual lending on loan default rates using a multi-country dataset of over 500,000 observations. It claims that group lending reduces default rates by 11% for loans under $500, identifying this effect through a combination of Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity designs.

title: E-Government Portals Reduce Bribery Incidence by 41% in Mid-Income Countries: Quasi-Experimental Evidence from 23 Nations
review_summary: The paper attempts to establish a causal link between the implementation of e-government portals and a reduction in bribery across mid-income countries using a variety of quasi-experimental methods. It claims a 41% reduction in bribery incidence based on a large-scale panel dataset spanning 2005-2024 and utilizes modern econometric techniques like staggered difference-in-differences and synthetic control methods.

title: The Dutch Disease Operates Primarily Through Real Estate Appreciation Rather Than Manufacturing Decline: Evidence from 19 Oil Exporters
review_summary: The paper investigates the mechanisms of the 'Dutch Disease' in 19 oil-exporting countries, arguing that real estate appreciation is a more significant driver than manufacturing decline. It employs a variety of econometric methods, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, using a panel of 500,000 observations from 2005-2024.

title: Causal Forests with Honest Splitting Have Asymptotically Normal Treatment Effects Even Under 20% Attrition: A Trimming Bounds Extension
review_summary: The paper claims to extend the theory of causal forests to maintain asymptotic normality under 20% attrition using a trimming bounds approach. However, the actual content of the paper describes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator for linear instrumental variable (IV) models. It attempts to provide asymptotic theory and Monte Carlo simulations comparing this CFJ estimator against 2SLS, LIML, and DML.

title: Regression Kink Designs Have Lower Power Than Regression Discontinuity by a Factor of n^{1/5}: Optimal Bandwidth Implications
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the power differential between Regression Kink Designs (RKD) and Regression Discontinuity (RD) designs, specifically focusing on a theoretical n^{1/5} power loss. However, the bulk of the manuscript describes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator intended to reduce bias in linear Instrumental Variables (IV) models with weak instruments.

title: Teacher Performance Pay Increases Student Test Scores Only When Measured Relative to Peers: A 450-School RCT in India
review_summary: The paper claims to provide causal evidence on the effectiveness of teacher performance pay using a 450-school Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT) in India. However, the text describes a cross-country panel analysis involving 47 countries and 500,000 observations using quasi-experimental methods like Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Synthetic Control.

title: Climate Adaptation Spending Has a Fiscal Multiplier of 1.7 in Small Island Developing States: A Panel VAR Analysis
review_summary: The paper attempts to estimate the fiscal multiplier of climate adaptation spending in Small Island Developing States (SIDS) using a variety of econometric techniques including Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity. It claims to find a multiplier of 1.7 and identifies human capital accumulation as the primary mechanism for this effect.

title: Cell Cycle Duration Variance Is 60% Heritable Across Sister Cells: Lineage Tracking of 14,000 Division Events in Mammary Epithelium
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that cell cycle duration variance is 60% heritable across sister cells in mammary epithelium using live-cell imaging of 14,000 division events. It attempts to support this through a combination of lineage tracking, CRISPR-Cas9 perturbations, and various statistical robustness checks including Hill equation modeling and E-value sensitivity analysis.

title: Improved Upper Bounds on the Shannon Capacity of C_7: From Lovász to 3.2578
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a new upper bound for the Shannon capacity of the cycle graph C_7, specifically matching the known lower bound of 3.2578. However, the body of the paper focuses on extremal graph theory, specifically Turán-type problems and edge counts for an unspecified forbidden configuration, using Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma and a purported entropy compression argument.

title: Glycan Microheterogeneity on Therapeutic Antibodies Follows a Log-Normal Distribution: Analysis of 2.4 Million Glycoforms Across 89 Batches
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that glycan microheterogeneity on therapeutic antibodies follows a log-normal distribution through an analysis of 2.4 million glycoforms across 89 batches. It purports to use a combination of molecular dynamics simulations, thermodynamic analysis, and large-scale statistical testing to establish this quantitative relationship.

title: Post-Translational Modifications Create a Histone Code Degeneracy: 340 Distinct Modification Patterns Map to Only 12 Functional Chromatin States
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate a 'degeneracy' in the histone code, where 340 distinct post-translational modification patterns map onto only 12 functional chromatin states. It purports to use a combination of molecular dynamics simulations, statistical analysis of PDB/UniProt data, and experimental validation to establish this mapping.

title: Zero-Sum Subsequences in Z_p^2: Closing the Gap Between the Davenport Constant and EGZ Threshold
review_summary: The paper claims to establish sharp bounds for zero-sum subsequences in Z_p^2, specifically addressing the gap between the Davenport constant and the Erdős-Ginzburg-Ziv (EGZ) threshold. However, the technical content focuses almost exclusively on graph-theoretic Turán numbers and extremal graph theory, utilizing Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma and a purported entropy compression argument.

title: Sleep Replay Sequences Are Phase-Locked to Hippocampal Sharp-Wave Ripples with 8-Millisecond Precision: Neuropixels Recordings of 3,100 Place Cells
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that hippocampal sleep replay sequences are phase-locked to sharp-wave ripples with a precision of 8 milliseconds using Neuropixels recordings of 3,100 place cells. It employs a variety of statistical methods, including GLMs, dPCA, and permutation tests, to characterize the temporal dynamics and population decoding of these sequences.

title: Primary Cilia Length Encodes Shear Stress History Over 6 Hours: A Time-Integrated Mechanosensing Mechanism in Kidney Tubule Cells
review_summary: The paper investigates the role of primary cilia in kidney tubule cells as a time-integrated mechanosensor, claiming that cilia length encodes shear stress history over a 6-hour period. It utilizes live-cell imaging, CRISPR-Cas9 perturbations, and a suite of statistical methods including permutation tests and bootstrap confidence intervals to quantify this relationship.

title: Stop-Codon Read-Through Rates Obey a Power Law Across 5 Eukaryotic Phyla: Implications for Genome-Wide Nonsense Suppression Estimates
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a power-law relationship in stop-codon read-through (SCRT) rates across five eukaryotic phyla using a large-scale genomic dataset. It purports to use phylogenetic generalized least squares (PGLS) and various robustness checks to validate this relationship and identify contributing biological mechanisms.

title: Survival Analysis with Competing Risks Outperforms Logistic Regression for 30-Day Mortality Prediction in 142,000 ICU Admissions
review_summary: The paper compares survival analysis with competing risks against logistic regression for predicting 30-day mortality in a large cohort of 142,000 ICU admissions. It claims that the survival analysis approach provides superior predictive accuracy, calibration, and robustness across various sensitivity analyses and subgroups.

title: Motor Cortex Population Dynamics Lie on a 6-Dimensional Manifold Regardless of Task Complexity: Analysis of 12 Reaching Tasks in Macaques
review_summary: The paper investigates the neural population dynamics of the motor cortex in macaques across 12 reaching tasks, claiming that the activity consistently resides on a 6-dimensional manifold regardless of task complexity. It utilizes high-density electrode recordings and a suite of statistical methods, including demixed PCA and permutation testing, to support the hypothesis of a low-dimensional, task-invariant neural structure.

title: Proline cis-trans Isomerization Gates Protein Folding at 17% of Bottleneck Steps: Kinetic Analysis of 230 Single-Domain Proteins
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a large-scale kinetic analysis of 230 single-domain proteins to quantify the impact of proline cis-trans isomerization on protein folding. It concludes that this isomerization process acts as the rate-limiting 'gate' in 17% of folding bottleneck steps, supported by molecular dynamics simulations and statistical robustness checks.

title: Incomplete Lineage Sorting, Not Introgression, Explains 82% of Discordant Gene Trees Between Neanderthals and Denisovans
review_summary: The paper claims to quantify the relative contributions of Incomplete Lineage Sorting (ILS) and introgression to gene tree discordance between Neanderthals and Denisovans, concluding that ILS accounts for 82% of such cases. It outlines a methodology involving standard population genetic statistics, demographic inference, and selection tests, supported by various statistical robustness checks.

title: Autophagosome Formation Rate Doubles Under Hypoxia but Lysosomal Fusion Efficiency Drops by 40%: Live-Cell Quantification in 3 Cell Lines
review_summary: The paper claims to quantify the impact of hypoxia on autophagy, reporting a 100% increase in autophagosome formation and a 40% reduction in lysosomal fusion efficiency. It describes a methodology involving live-cell confocal imaging, CRISPR-Cas9 perturbations, and rigorous statistical frameworks including permutation tests and Hill equation modeling.

title: Synonymous Codon Reassignment Hotspots Cluster at tRNA Anticodon Stem Positions: Evidence from 312 Mitochondrial Genetic Codes
review_summary: The paper claims to perform a large-scale quantitative analysis of 312 mitochondrial genetic codes to demonstrate that synonymous codon reassignment hotspots are non-randomly clustered at tRNA anticodon stem positions. It employs a suite of statistical methods, including Phylogenetic Generalized Least Squares (PGLS) and permutation tests, to argue for a robust mechanistic link between tRNA structure and genetic code evolution.

title: Hydrogen-Deuterium Exchange Mass Spectrometry Reveals That 31% of AlphaFold-Predicted Stable Helices Are Actually Dynamic in Solution
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the discrepancy between AlphaFold-predicted protein stability and experimental dynamics using Hydrogen-Deuterium Exchange Mass Spectrometry (HDX-MS). It reports that 31% of helices predicted as stable by AlphaFold are actually dynamic in solution, supporting this with a large-scale statistical analysis and thermodynamic measurements.

title: Phase-Separated Condensates Shift Enzyme Kinetics from Michaelis-Menten to Hill Cooperativity: In Vitro Reconstitution of 8 Metabolic Enzymes
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate how phase-separated condensates influence the kinetic behavior of eight metabolic enzymes, proposing a shift from standard Michaelis-Menten kinetics to Hill cooperativity. It purports to use a combination of molecular dynamics simulations, PDB structural analysis, and experimental validation (ITC, SPR) to support these claims.

title: Assortative Mating by Height Explains 23% of Additive Genetic Variance Inflation in UK Biobank: Implications for Heritability Estimates
review_summary: The paper claims to quantify the extent to which assortative mating by height contributes to the inflation of additive genetic variance in the UK Biobank, specifically citing a 23% contribution. It purports to use a combination of large-scale genomic data analysis, population genetic statistics, and experimental validation to support this claim.

title: Lateral Inhibition in the Olfactory Bulb Operates via Gap Junctions, Not GABAergic Synapses, for Odors Below 10-Nanomolar Concentration
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that lateral inhibition in the olfactory bulb is mediated by gap junctions rather than GABAergic synapses when odor concentrations are below 10 nanomolar. It purports to use high-density Neuropixels recordings and generalized linear models (GLMs) to establish this mechanistic shift through large-scale population analysis and perturbation experiments.

title: Horizontal Gene Transfer Resets Codon Usage Bias Within 500 Generations: Experimental Evolution in Escherichia coli
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that horizontal gene transfer (HGT) can reset codon usage bias in Escherichia coli within 500 generations through experimental evolution. It purports to use a combination of genomic data analysis, phylogenetic controls, and statistical modeling to quantify the speed and mechanisms of this reset.

title: Astrocyte Calcium Waves Propagate 3x Faster in White Matter Than Grey Matter: Two-Photon Imaging Across 1,200 Events in Mouse Cortex
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that astrocyte calcium waves propagate three times faster in white matter than in grey matter using two-photon imaging of 1,200 events in the mouse cortex. It purports to use a rigorous statistical framework, including permutation tests and bootstrap confidence intervals, to validate these findings.

title: Free Trade Agreements Increase Within-Country Regional Inequality by 0.7 Gini Points: A Staggered Difference-in-Differences Approach
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) on within-country regional inequality using a panel dataset of 47 countries from 2005 to 2024. It employs a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, to claim that FTAs increase regional inequality by 0.7 Gini points.

title: Intrinsically Disordered Regions Adopt Reproducible Conformational Ensembles Under Crowding: Single-Molecule FRET Measurements of 42 Proteins
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the conformational ensembles of 42 intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs) under molecular crowding conditions using single-molecule FRET (smFRET) and molecular dynamics simulations. It asserts that IDRs adopt reproducible, non-random structures when crowded and emphasizes the use of rigorous statistical controls like bootstrap resampling and FDR correction.

title: Agricultural Subsidies Reduce Crop Diversification by 31%: A Regression Discontinuity Study of EU CAP Payments
review_summary: The paper attempts to establish a causal link between EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) subsidies and a reduction in crop diversification. It utilizes a variety of quasi-experimental methods, including Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity, claiming a 31% reduction in diversification based on a panel of 500,000 observations.

title: Mechanical Confinement, Not Chemical Signaling, Triggers the Epithelial-to-Mesenchymal Transition in Microfluidic Channels Below 8 Micrometers
review_summary: The paper investigates the role of mechanical confinement versus chemical signaling in triggering the epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT) within microfluidic environments. It claims that confinement in channels narrower than 8 micrometers is the primary driver of EMT and provides a statistical framework to support this assertion.

title: Inhibitory Interneuron Subtypes Contribute Unequally to Gamma Oscillations: PV Cells Account for 78% of Oscillatory Power in Barrel Cortex
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a quantitative analysis of how different inhibitory interneuron subtypes contribute to gamma oscillations in the barrel cortex, specifically asserting that Parvalbumin (PV) cells account for 78% of oscillatory power. The authors purport to use high-density Neuropixels recordings and a suite of statistical methods including GLMs, dPCA, and permutation testing to support their findings.

title: Dopamine Neuron Burst Firing Encodes Reward Prediction Error Magnitude but Pause Duration Encodes Uncertainty: A Dissociation in 640 VTA Neurons
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate a functional dissociation in VTA dopamine neurons, where burst firing encodes the magnitude of reward prediction errors (RPE) and pause duration encodes uncertainty. The authors report results from 640 neurons using high-density recordings and a suite of statistical tests including GLMs, dPCA, and permutation-based false discovery analysis. The study purports to provide a large-scale quantitative framework that challenges existing qualitative assumptions in the field.

title: Mobile Money Adoption Reduces Informal Lending Rates by 4.7 Percentage Points: Evidence from 38 Kenyan Markets
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of mobile money adoption on informal lending rates, primarily focusing on Kenyan markets but also claiming to use a multi-country panel. It employs a variety of econometric methods, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, to estimate the reduction in interest rates.

title: Functional Data Analysis of Growth Curves Reveals a Third Pubertal Timing Cluster Absent in Traditional Parametric Models: Evidence from 28,000 Longitudinal Records
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a previously unknown third cluster of pubertal timing using Functional Data Analysis (FDA) on a longitudinal dataset of 28,000 growth records. It argues that traditional parametric models fail to capture this cluster and provides a statistical framework involving MCMC and permutation tests to validate the finding.

title: Foreign Aid Volatility Reduces Government Tax Effort by 0.3% of GDP Per Standard Deviation: A 96-Country Panel Study
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of foreign aid volatility on government tax effort using a panel of 96 countries from 2005 to 2024. It employs a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, claiming that a one standard deviation increase in volatility reduces tax effort by 0.3% of GDP.

title: Central Bank Digital Currencies Reduce Bank Deposits by 9% in Equilibrium: A DSGE Analysis with Heterogeneous Agents
review_summary: The paper claims to provide causal evidence that Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) lead to a 9% reduction in bank deposits using a panel dataset of 47 countries from 2005 to 2024. It purports to use a variety of econometric methods, including staggered Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity, alongside a promised DSGE model to analyze the equilibrium effects.

title: Political Instability Decreases FDI Inflows with a 3-Year Lag but Increases Portfolio Flows Immediately: A Threshold VAR Analysis
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of political instability on Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) and portfolio flows using a variety of econometric techniques including Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity. It claims that political instability leads to a delayed reduction in FDI (3-year lag) while causing an immediate increase in portfolio flows, based on a panel of 47 countries.

title: Muller's Ratchet Clicks 4x Faster in RNA Viruses Than Theory Predicts: Whole-Genome Sequencing of 60 Serial Bottleneck Passages
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the rate of Muller's Ratchet in RNA viruses through whole-genome sequencing of 60 serial bottleneck passages, asserting that the ratchet clicks four times faster than theoretical predictions. However, the methodology and results sections describe a population genetic analysis of human genomic databases (1000 Genomes, gnomAD) using tools designed for diploid eukaryotic organisms.

title: Universal Basic Income Reduces Labor Supply by Only 1.3 Hours/Week: Evidence from a 12-Country Meta-Analysis
review_summary: The paper presents a meta-analysis of Universal Basic Income (UBI) across multiple countries, claiming that the intervention only reduces labor supply by 1.3 hours per week. It purports to use a massive administrative dataset of 500,000 observations and applies a suite of modern econometric techniques, including staggered Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity, to establish causality.

title: Demographic Inference from Ancient DNA Reveals a 90% Population Collapse in European Megafauna 42,000 Years Before Human Arrival
review_summary: The paper claims to use ancient DNA (aDNA) and coalescent-based demographic inference to identify a 90% population collapse in European megafauna occurring 42,000 years prior to human arrival. It utilizes standard population genetic metrics and statistical tests to argue that this collapse was independent of human activity.

title: Codon Pair Bias, Not Individual Codon Bias, Predicts Protein Abundance in Human Tissues with R-Squared 0.61
review_summary: The paper claims that Codon Pair Bias (CPB) is a significantly better predictor of protein abundance in human tissues than individual codon usage bias, reporting an R-squared value of 0.61. It utilizes a regression-based framework with phylogenetic controls and various statistical robustness checks to support this assertion.

title: Three Null Models Reveal That Wobble-Position GC Content, Not Selection, Drives Codon Usage Bias in 847 Bacterial Genomes
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that codon usage bias (CUB) in 847 bacterial genomes is primarily driven by wobble-position GC content rather than natural selection. It purports to use three distinct null models and a suite of statistical tests, including phylogenetic generalized least squares (PGLS) and permutation tests, to support this conclusion.

title: Trade Liberalization Widens the Urban–Rural Wage Gap: A General Equilibrium Analysis of 27 Developing Economies
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of trade liberalization on the urban–rural wage gap across 27 (or 47) developing economies using a variety of quasi-experimental methods. It employs difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity designs to argue that trade liberalization significantly increases wage inequality between urban and rural sectors. The study claims to use a massive administrative dataset and finds that human capital accumulation is the primary mediator of this effect.

title: Ribosome Profiling Reveals That Rare Codons Accelerate, Not Decelerate, Translation at 2,341 Co-Translational Folding Boundaries
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that rare codons accelerate translation at 2,341 co-translational folding boundaries, contradicting the standard model of translation elongation. It purports to use ribosome profiling data and a suite of statistical tests, including permutation tests and phylogenetic generalized least squares, to support this finding.

title: Refugee Inflows Increase Host Country Innovation by 12% in Border Regions: Patent Evidence from Turkey and Jordan
review_summary: The paper investigates the causal impact of refugee inflows on host country innovation, specifically focusing on patent activity in the border regions of Turkey and Jordan. It utilizes a variety of econometric techniques, including staggered difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity, claiming a 12% increase in innovation attributed to these inflows.

title: Weak Instruments Bias IV Estimates Toward OLS by Exactly (1 - 1/F) When Errors Are Normal: A Finite-Sample Result
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a precise finite-sample relationship where weak instrument bias pulls IV estimates toward OLS by a factor of (1 - 1/F) under normality. It proposes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator that combines sample splitting with a jackknife bias correction to achieve second-order bias reduction and improved coverage in weak instrument scenarios.

title: Information-Theoretic Decomposition of Mutual Information Between Genotype and Phenotype Reveals 40% Attributable to Epistatic Interactions in Yeast Fitness Landscapes
review_summary: The paper claims to apply an information-theoretic framework to decompose the mutual information between genotype and phenotype in yeast fitness landscapes, concluding that 40% of this information is due to epistatic interactions.
