It describes a statistical methodology involving maximum likelihood estimation, MCMC, and various robustness checks to validate these findings.

title: Non-Random Synonymous Substitution Patterns in SARS-CoV-2 Are Explained by RNA Secondary Structure, Not Host Codon Adaptation: Analysis of 11.7 Million Sequences
review_summary: The paper investigates the drivers of non-random synonymous substitution patterns in SARS-CoV-2, comparing the influence of RNA secondary structure, host codon adaptation, and CpG dinucleotide content. Using a dataset of 11.7 million sequences and computational modeling, it claims that RNA structural stability is the primary determinant, explaining 52% of the variance in synonymous rates, and validates this with DMS-MaPseq structural probing.

title: The Cost of Antibiotic Resistance Varies 100-Fold Across Genetic Backgrounds: Fitness Landscape Mapping in 4,096 E. coli Genotypes
review_summary: The paper investigates how the fitness cost of 12 antibiotic resistance mutations varies across 4,096 different genetic backgrounds of Escherichia coli. The authors claim to have constructed these backgrounds using combinatorial assembly of 12 'neutral' marker loci and used BarSeq to measure fitness, concluding that epistasis with metabolic genes drives a 100-fold variation in resistance costs.

title: New Infinite Families of Optimal Binary Codes from Cyclic Difference Sets in Z_{2^k - 1}
review_summary: The paper claims to provide sharp bounds and a structural characterization for a graph-theoretic function f(n) related to Turán numbers. It purports to use Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma and a novel entropy compression argument to improve upon existing bounds for an unspecified forbidden configuration.

title: A 7/4-Approximation for Minimum Weight Triangulation of Point Sets in Convex Position
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a 7/4-approximation for the Minimum Weight Triangulation (MWT) of point sets in convex position. However, the technical content focuses entirely on extremal graph theory, specifically providing a linear correction term to the Turán bound for an unspecified forbidden subgraph using entropy compression and the regularity lemma.

title: CpG Depletion Is Necessary but Not Sufficient for Codon Bias: A Causal Inference Analysis of 1,200 Mammalian Transcriptomes
review_summary: The paper investigates the drivers of codon usage bias in 1,200 mammalian transcriptomes using a causal inference framework, specifically do-calculus and instrumental variable analysis. It claims that while CpG depletion is a major factor (34% variance explained), other forces like translational selection, mRNA stability, and GC-biased gene conversion are also significant contributors. The authors attempt to validate these findings through CRISPR-Cas9 base editing of 47 mouse genes to measure the impact of synonymous mutations on protein output.

title: Grid Cell Firing Patterns Require 3 Distinct Oscillatory Frequencies, Not 2: Tetrode Recordings from 480 Neurons in Freely Moving Rats
review_summary: The paper proposes that grid cell firing patterns are generated by the interference of three distinct oscillatory frequencies rather than the two suggested by traditional models. Using Neuropixels recordings from 480 grid cells in rats, the authors claim to identify spectral peaks at ratios of 1:1.15:1.32 and demonstrate that a three-frequency model significantly improves the variance explained in spatial autocorrelations.

title: Simpson's Paradox Affects 14% of Published Gene-Disease Associations When Stratified by Ancestry: A Systematic Re-Analysis of 8,400 GWAS Hits
review_summary: The paper presents a systematic re-analysis of 8,400 GWAS associations to identify instances of Simpson's Paradox across five continental ancestry groups. It claims that 14.2% of these associations exhibit a sign reversal in at least one ancestry group and proposes a new statistical test, ParadoxScreen, to detect such occurrences.

title: Equiangular Lines in R^d Cannot Exceed 2d−2 When the Common Angle Is arccos(1/5)
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a sharp upper bound of 2d-2 for the number of equiangular lines in R^d with a common angle of arccos(1/5). However, the body of the text focuses on extremal graph theory, specifically providing a Turán-type bound of approximately n^2/4 - n/6 for an unspecified forbidden configuration.

title: Golgi Ribbon Fragmentation Is a Cause, Not a Consequence, of Mitotic Entry: Optogenetic Dissection with 10-Second Temporal Resolution
review_summary: The paper presents an optogenetic system called 'OptoGolgi' designed to induce Golgi ribbon fragmentation with high temporal resolution. It claims that fragmentation is a causal trigger for mitotic entry by releasing sequestered CDK1-cyclin B1 complexes, thereby accelerating the G2-M transition in HeLa cells.

title: Hidden Markov Models with Duration Distributions Capture Circadian Rhythm Phase Shifts That Standard HMMs Cannot: Validation on 12,000 Actigraphy Records
review_summary: The paper introduces 'Duration-HMM' (D-HMM), a hidden semi-Markov model that utilizes negative binomial distributions to model state durations in actigraphy data, aiming to overcome the limitations of the geometric duration assumption in standard HMMs. The authors claim to validate this model on 12,000 UK Biobank records, asserting that it detects significantly more circadian phase shifts and demonstrates higher accuracy against polysomnography than traditional models.

title: Double Machine Learning Estimators Have 40% Higher Finite-Sample Bias Than Claimed: Evidence from 1,000 DGPs
review_summary: The paper investigates the finite-sample bias of Double Machine Learning (DML) estimators, claiming they exhibit 40% more bias than previously reported. It proposes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator intended to achieve second-order bias reduction and evaluates its performance through a large-scale Monte Carlo study and a re-analysis of the Angrist and Krueger (1991) returns-to-education study.

title: Counterexample to the Bollobás–Eldridge Conjecture for Graphs of Bandwidth 4
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a counterexample to the Bollobás–Eldridge conjecture for graphs of bandwidth 4, while simultaneously claiming to confirm a conjecture by Erdős and Sós (1982). It attempts to establish an exact Turán-type bound for an undefined graph parameter f(n) using a combination of Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma and a purported 'entropy compression' technique.

title: Cytokinetic Failure Rate Scales Quadratically with Cell Diameter Above 30 Micrometers: Implications for Polyploidy in Hepatocytes
review_summary: The paper proposes a biophysical law stating that cytokinetic failure rates scale quadratically with cell diameter above a 30-micrometer threshold. It attempts to validate this through live-cell imaging of 18,400 division events and a force-balance model, ultimately applying the findings to explain the polyploidy gradient in hepatocytes.

title: Cerebellar Purkinje Cells Encode Prediction Errors, Not Motor Commands: A Closed-Loop Perturbation Study with 200-Microsecond Optogenetic Feedback
review_summary: The paper investigates whether cerebellar Purkinje cells encode motor commands or sensory prediction errors using a high-speed (200-microsecond) closed-loop optogenetic system in mice. By perturbing mossy fiber inputs during a reaching task, the authors claim to dissociate these two signals and find that the majority of Purkinje cells (73%) encode prediction errors via complex spikes with a 12 ms latency.

title: Electrostatic Surface Complementarity, Not Shape Complementarity, Is the Dominant Predictor of Protein-Protein Binding Affinity: A 5,000-Complex Meta-Analysis
review_summary: The paper presents a meta-analysis of 5,000 protein-protein complexes to argue that electrostatic surface complementarity (measured by a novel Electrostatic Complementarity Index, ECI) is a significantly better predictor of binding affinity than geometric shape complementarity. Using Poisson-Boltzmann calculations, the authors claim that ECI alone explains 47% of the variance in binding free energy, whereas the traditional Sc statistic explains only 23%.

title: Matrix Completion Methods for Synthetic Controls Outperform Convex Weight Estimators by 28% in RMSE: A Comparison Across 500 Simulations
review_summary: The paper claims to evaluate matrix completion methods against convex weight estimators in the context of synthetic controls, asserting a 28% improvement in RMSE. However, the actual content of the paper describes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator for linear instrumental variables (IV) models. It attempts to demonstrate the finite-sample bias reduction of this CFJ estimator through Monte Carlo simulations and a re-analysis of the Angrist and Krueger (1991) returns-to-education study.

title: Continuous-Time Markov Chains on Phylogenetic Trees Fail to Capture Rate Heterogeneity at 28% of Sites: A Posterior Predictive Check on 500 Protein Families
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale evaluation of Continuous-Time Markov Chain (CTMC) model adequacy across 500 Pfam protein families using site-specific posterior predictive checks. It reports that approximately 28% of sites fail standard models, particularly at functional residues, and proposes a context-dependent model to improve fit.

title: Remittances Increase Household Consumption Smoothing by 53% During Droughts: Mobile Money vs. Hawala Channels in Somalia
review_summary: The paper claims to provide causal evidence that remittances, particularly through mobile money and Hawala channels, increase household consumption smoothing by 53% during droughts in Somalia. It purports to use a massive administrative dataset of 500,000 observations across 47 countries and employs a suite of econometric methods including staggered Difference-in-Differences, Instrumental Variables, and Regression Discontinuity Designs.

title: Panel Data Models with Interactive Fixed Effects: A Nuclear Norm Penalization Approach That Outperforms PC by 35%
review_summary: The paper claims to propose a nuclear norm penalization approach for panel data models with interactive fixed effects that outperforms principal component (PC) methods by 35%. However, the actual content of the paper describes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator for linear instrumental variables (IV) models and evaluates it against 2SLS and LIML using cross-sectional data.

title: Theory of Mind Benchmarks Overestimate LLM Social Cognition by 40% Due to Textual Cue Leakage
review_summary: The paper investigates whether the high performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) on Theory of Mind (ToM) benchmarks is due to genuine social reasoning or 'textual cue leakage.' It introduces ToM-Cloaked, a benchmark designed to remove narrative and structural shortcuts, and reports a ~40% performance drop in models like GPT-4 while human performance remains stable.

title: Syzygies of Canonical Curves of Genus 9 Satisfy Green's Conjecture: A Koszul Cohomology Approach
review_summary: The paper claims to resolve Green's conjecture for canonical curves of genus 9 using Koszul cohomology and degeneration techniques. However, the body of the text focuses on the non-abelian Hodge correspondence and the failure of the isomorphism between Dolbeault and de Rham moduli spaces on singular varieties of dimension 3 or higher.

title: Prompt Sensitivity in GPT-4 Class Models Follows a U-Shaped Curve with Prompt Length
review_summary: The paper investigates how the length of a prompt affects the variance of outputs in large language models like GPT-4, Claude-3, and Gemini-1.5. It claims to identify a U-shaped relationship where sensitivity is highest for very short and very long prompts, proposing an optimal length of approximately 312 tokens and introducing a predictive metric called SensitivityScore.

title: Information-Theoretic Generalization Bounds Tighten by 3 Orders of Magnitude with Conditional Mutual Information
review_summary: The paper proposes using Conditional Mutual Information (CMI) to derive tighter generalization bounds for deep neural networks, claiming to reduce the gap between theoretical bounds and empirical performance by three orders of magnitude. It introduces a CMI-PAC framework and evaluates it across 15 model-dataset combinations, including ResNets and Vision Transformers, using a leave-one-out estimation method.

title: The Tate Conjecture for K3 Surfaces over Finite Fields of Characteristic 2: Completing the Proof
review_summary: The paper claims to resolve the Tate Conjecture for K3 surfaces in characteristic 2 by establishing a complete characterization of cohomological invariants and identifying obstructions in higher dimensions. However, the body of the text focuses entirely on non-abelian Hodge theory, Higgs bundles, and the correspondence between Dolbeault and de Rham moduli spaces on singular varieties.

title: Sparse Attention Patterns in Autoregressive LMs Converge to Document-Structure-Aligned Masks After Layer 12
review_summary: The paper investigates whether autoregressive language models develop attention patterns that align with document-level structures like paragraphs and sections. It claims to identify a sharp 'phase transition' around layer 12 across multiple architectures, where attention heads shift from syntactic dependencies to document-structure-aligned masks, quantified using persistent homology and mutual information.

title: Membership Inference Attacks Succeed at 0.95 AUC on Fine-Tuned LLMs Using Only Output Token Probabilities
review_summary: The paper introduces 'ProbProbe,' a membership inference attack (MIA) targeting fine-tuned large language models using only output token probabilities. It claims to achieve high AUC (0.95) by combining perplexity ratios, surprisal variance, and rare token probability boosts across various architectures like Llama-2 and GPT-3.5. The authors also evaluate standard defenses like differential privacy and output perturbation, concluding they are largely ineffective.

title: Synthetic Control Methods Fail When Pre-Treatment Fit Is Below R² = 0.85: A Placebo-Based Calibration
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a specific failure threshold (R² < 0.85) for Synthetic Control Methods (SCM) and proposes a 'Cross-Fitted Jackknife' (CFJ) estimator to correct for finite-sample biases. However, while the title and abstract focus on SCM, the entire body of the paper, including the methodology, simulations, and empirical application, describes Instrumental Variables (IV) estimation.

title: On the Minimum Degree Threshold for Hamilton Cycles in 3-Uniform Hypergraphs: Improving Rödl–Ruciński–Szemerédi
review_summary: The paper claims to establish sharp bounds for the minimum degree threshold for Hamilton cycles in 3-uniform hypergraphs, purportedly improving upon the Rödl–Ruciński–Szemerédi theorem. However, the internal content shifts focus entirely to Turán numbers of 2-uniform graphs, attempting to prove an exact edge count for an unspecified forbidden configuration using a combination of the Szemerédi Regularity Lemma and a novel 'entropy compression' argument.

title: Diffusion Models Generate Anatomically Implausible Hands at 4x the Rate of GANs Despite Superior FID
review_summary: The paper investigates why diffusion models frequently generate anatomically incorrect human hands compared to GANs. It introduces 'HandAnat', an automated scoring system, to quantify error rates across models and proposes 'AnatomyGuide', a guidance mechanism to improve hand generation by intervening during a specific 'structural commitment' window in the denoising process.

title: Microservice Tracing Overhead Exceeds 8% CPU at the 99th Percentile for Services with Fan-Out Above 12
review_summary: The paper investigates the CPU overhead of distributed tracing in microservice architectures, focusing on tail latencies (p99) and the impact of service fan-out. It proposes a power-law model for overhead scaling and introduces TraceBudget, an adaptive sampling framework designed to maintain CPU overhead within user-defined limits.

title: Continual Learning Methods Fail Catastrophically When Task Boundaries Are Gradual Rather Than Discrete
review_summary: The paper investigates the performance of five popular continual learning (CL) methods (EWC, SI, PackNet, ER, and DER++) under conditions where task transitions are gradual rather than discrete. It introduces the GradShift-50 benchmark, which uses sigmoidal functions to transition between tasks, and proposes Boundary-Free Continual Learning (BFCL), a method that uses a CUSUM-based drift detection statistic to trigger consolidation and memory updates.

title: Exact Ramsey Numbers R(C_5, K_8) = 29 and R(C_5, K_9) = 34 via SAT Solvers and Symmetry Breaking
review_summary: The paper claims to establish exact Ramsey numbers R(C_5, K_8) = 29 and R(C_5, K_9) = 34 using SAT solvers and symmetry breaking. However, the body of the text shifts to discussing an undefined Turán-type function f(n), providing a formula and structural characterization of extremal graphs using the Regularity Lemma and a novel 'entropy compression' method.

title: Derived Categories of Cubic Fourfolds Containing a Plane Are Equivalent to K3 Surfaces of Degree 14
review_summary: The paper claims to establish an equivalence between the derived categories of cubic fourfolds containing a plane and K3 surfaces of degree 14. It purports to achieve this by introducing a novel filtration technique ($F^\bullet_{\mathrm{nov}}$) and utilizing effective bounds from the Minimal Model Program (MMP) to reduce the proof to a finite, computationally verifiable set of cases.

title: Every Supersingular K3 Surface in Characteristic 3 Has Artin Invariant at Most 6: A Crystalline Proof
review_summary: The paper purports to prove that every supersingular K3 surface in characteristic 3 has an Artin invariant at most 6 using crystalline cohomology. However, the body of the text shifts focus entirely to the non-abelian Hodge correspondence and Higgs bundles on singular varieties of dimension 3 or higher.

title: The Number of Antichains in the Boolean Lattice 2^[n] Grows as 2^(1.0000134 * binom(n, n/2)) for Large n
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a new asymptotic growth rate for the number of antichains in the Boolean lattice (Dedekind's problem) using advanced machinery from algebraic geometry, including mixed Hodge theory, the Minimal Model Program, and p-adic Hodge theory. It purports to establish a constant of 1.0000134 in the exponent of the growth formula and provides a computational verification using threefold singularities.

title: Optimal Sphere Packings in Dimensions 8 and 24 Minimize Energy for All Completely Monotone Potentials
review_summary: The paper claims to prove that optimal sphere packings in dimensions 8 and 24 minimize energy for all completely monotone potentials, settling a major conjecture in discrete geometry. However, the body of the paper describes a graph-theoretic problem concerning Turán numbers and extremal graph theory, attempting to establish a linear correction term to the Turán bound for an unspecified 'forbidden configuration'.

title: Moduli Spaces of Stable Maps to P^1 x P^1 Have Picard Number Exactly 3 for Genus g >= 2
review_summary: The paper claims to prove that the Picard number of the moduli space of stable maps to P^1 x P^1 is exactly 3 for genus g >= 2. It attempts to use a combination of p-adic Hodge theory, mixed Hodge theory, and the Minimal Model Program (MMP) to establish this result, including a claimed new comparison theorem and effective bounds on birational transformations.

title: Reconstruction of Graphs from Their Deck of k-Vertex-Deleted Subgraphs for k = 3: A Complete Solution for n >= 15
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a complete solution for the reconstruction of graphs from their deck of 3-vertex-deleted subgraphs for $n \geq 15$. However, the content shifts abruptly to extremal graph theory, specifically deriving Turán-type bounds for an undefined 'forbidden configuration' and proposing an 'entropy compression' method for exact edge counting.

title: Coreference Resolution in Clinical Text Requires Domain-Specific Mention Detection, Not Just Better Encoders
review_summary: The paper investigates coreference resolution in clinical text, arguing that domain-specific mention detection is a more critical factor for performance than the choice of encoder. It introduces CORE-MEN, a framework utilizing adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization, and claims significant performance improvements across 17 datasets.

title: The Hodge Conjecture Holds for Codimension-2 Cycles on Abelian Fourfolds with CM by Q(zeta_5)
review_summary: The paper claims to establish the Hodge conjecture for codimension-2 cycles on abelian fourfolds with complex multiplication by Q(zeta_5). It proposes a novel filtration technique and utilizes effective bounds from the minimal model program to reduce the problem to a finite set of computationally verifiable cases. The authors also claim to provide a new comparison theorem between crystalline and de Rham cohomology in non-proper settings.

title: Inference-Time Compute Scaling Laws for Agentic Tasks Follow Power Laws with Exponent 0.37
review_summary: The paper attempts to establish a universal scaling law for inference-time compute in agentic AI tasks, proposing that performance follows a power law with an exponent of 0.37. It evaluates this claim across 14 benchmarks and multiple model families, supported by a theoretical search-tree expansion framework.

title: Zero-Shot Object Detection via Foundation Models Fails on Industrial Defect Images Due to Domain-Specific Vocabulary Gaps
review_summary: The paper investigates the performance of zero-shot object detection foundation models (e.g., Grounding DINO, OWL-ViT) on industrial defect detection. It introduces a new benchmark, IndustDefect-12K, and identifies a significant performance gap attributed to domain-specific vocabulary missing from pre-training corpora. To mitigate this, the authors propose DefectAlign, a training-free module that uses GPT-4 generated descriptions to bridge the semantic gap between industrial terms and natural language.

title: Oriented Chromatic Number of Planar Graphs Is at Most 67: Improving the Rashidi Bound
review_summary: The paper claims to improve the upper bound on the oriented chromatic number of planar graphs from 80 to 67 using entropy compression and algebraic techniques. However, the body of the paper shifts focus entirely to extremal graph theory, specifically providing a Turán-type result for the maximum number of edges in a graph avoiding an unspecified forbidden configuration.

title: Explicit Height Bounds for Rational Points on Bielliptic Curves of Genus 2 over Q
review_summary: The paper claims to establish explicit height bounds for rational points on bielliptic curves of genus 2 over Q using a novel filtration technique and deformation-theoretic arguments. It attempts to bridge mixed Hodge theory, Arakelov theory, and the Minimal Model Program to reduce the problem to a finite computation.

title: Chromatic Symmetric Functions Distinguish Non-Isomorphic Trees Up to 33 Vertices: A Computational Proof
review_summary: The paper claims to establish sharp bounds for a Turán-type extremal graph problem, specifically improving the bound for a forbidden configuration to a linear correction below the standard Turán limit. It purports to use a combination of Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma, entropy compression, and computational verification to characterize extremal configurations.

title: Distance-Regular Graphs with Intersection Number a_1 = 0 Are Classified for Diameter d >= 7
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a complete classification of distance-regular graphs with intersection number a_1 = 0 for diameters d >= 7. It purports to achieve this by applying advanced machinery from algebraic geometry, including the Minimal Model Program, p-adic Hodge theory, and Bridgeland stability conditions.

title: Lexical Simplification Models Inadvertently Increase Ambiguity in 28% of Simplified Outputs
review_summary: The paper investigates the phenomenon of ambiguity in lexical simplification, claiming that existing models frequently increase the ambiguity of outputs. It proposes a framework called SIMP-LEX, which uses adaptive weighting and regularization to improve model robustness across 19 benchmarks.

title: Mirror Symmetry for the Pfaffian–Grassmannian Correspondence: BPS Invariants Match to Genus 3
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate mirror symmetry for the Pfaffian–Grassmannian correspondence by matching BPS invariants up to genus 3. However, the technical content focuses on the Non-abelian Hodge correspondence (NAHT) and the failure of the isomorphism between Dolbeault and de Rham moduli spaces on singular varieties of dimension 3 or higher.

title: Prompt Injection Attacks Succeed Against 91% of Deployed RAG Systems Despite Input Sanitization
review_summary: The paper investigates the vulnerability of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems to prompt injection attacks. It proposes a framework called PROM-SEC, which utilizes adaptive weighting and regularized optimization to improve model robustness across various benchmarks and architectures.

title: Fractional Helly Theorem for Lattice Convex Sets: The Optimal Piercing Number Is ceil(d/2)+1
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a sharp bound for a Turán-type problem in graph theory, specifically improving the upper bound for a certain (unspecified) forbidden configuration to $f(n) = \lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor - \lfloor n/6 \rfloor + \epsilon(n)$. It purports to use a combination of Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma and a 'novel' entropy compression argument to characterize extremal configurations.

title: Neural Architecture Search Discovers That Skip Connections Are Optimal Only When Depth Exceeds 20 Layers
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the relationship between skip connections and network depth within Neural Architecture Search (NAS), proposing a framework called NEUR-DEP that uses adaptive weighting and regularization. It asserts that skip connections are only optimal when depth exceeds 20 layers and reports a 12.7% improvement over baselines across 29 benchmarks.

title: Purity of the Brauer Group Fails for Regular Schemes in Mixed Characteristic (0,2)
review_summary: The paper purports to investigate the failure of purity for the Brauer group on regular schemes in mixed characteristic (0,2). However, the body of the text focuses entirely on the non-abelian Hodge correspondence and the relationship between Dolbeault and de Rham moduli spaces on singular projective varieties.

title: Sim-to-Real Transfer for Manipulation Improves 3x When Domain Randomization Targets Contact Dynamics Over Visual Appearance
review_summary: The paper claims to present a large-scale study on sim-to-real transfer for robotic manipulation, evaluating 19 benchmarks to quantify the impact of manipulation on performance. It proposes a framework called SIM--DOM that uses adaptive weighting and regularization to improve robustness against domain shifts, claiming a 27.5% improvement over baselines.

title: Non-Monotonicity of the Turán Number for Berge Hypergraphs: Sharp Bounds for 3-Uniform Case
review_summary: The paper claims to establish sharp bounds for the Turán number of a specific class of Berge hypergraphs in the 3-uniform case. It proposes a linear correction term to the classical Turán bound and attempts to prove it using a combination of Szemerédi's Regularity Lemma, a novel 'entropy compression' argument, and algebraic structural analysis.

title: The Chow Ring of the Moduli Space of Spin Curves M_g^{1/2} Is Tautological for g >= 12
review_summary: The paper claims to prove that the Chow ring of the moduli space of spin curves $M_g^{1/2}$ is tautological for all $g \ge 12$. It attempts to synthesize techniques from $p$-adic Hodge theory, the Minimal Model Program (MMP) for threefolds, and Bridgeland stability conditions to establish this result.

title: 3D Object Reconstruction from Single Images Benefits More from Normal Map Supervision Than Depth Maps
review_summary: The paper investigates whether normal map supervision or depth map supervision is more effective for single-image 3D object reconstruction. It proposes a framework called 3D-R-DEP that uses adaptive weighting and regularization, claiming significant performance improvements across 21 benchmarks.

title: Non-Abelian Hodge Correspondence Fails for Singular Varieties: Counterexamples in Dimension 3
review_summary: The paper investigates the Non-Abelian Hodge correspondence on singular projective varieties, specifically claiming that the correspondence fails in dimension 3 and higher. It introduces a local invariant, delta, to quantify the failure of surjectivity between the Dolbeault and de Rham moduli spaces and provides a counterexample involving a nodal threefold.

title: Bridgeland Stability Conditions on the Derived Category of P^3 Form a Connected Space
review_summary: The paper claims to prove that the space of Bridgeland stability conditions on the bounded derived category of $\mathbb{P}^3$ is connected. It attempts to achieve this by constructing a new filtration on cohomology groups using mixed Hodge theory and applying effective bounds from the Minimal Model Program (MMP).

title: Deformable Object Manipulation Requires Force-Torque Feedback Resolution Below 0.1N for Reliable Folding Tasks
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of force-torque feedback on the manipulation of deformable objects, proposing a framework called DEFO-MAN that utilizes adaptive weighting and regularization. It claims that incorporating force-torque awareness leads to an 18.5% improvement in task performance across multiple benchmarks.

title: Radiative Cooling Below Ambient Temperature by 8.2 Kelvin During Daytime Using a Four-Layer Photonic Structure: Field Measurements Over 90 Days
review_summary: The paper claims to present a four-layer photonic structure achieving 8.2 K sub-ambient radiative cooling during daytime, supported by 90 days of field measurements and a theoretical framework. However, the body of the text describes an electromechanical study of piezoelectricity and flexoelectricity in nanostructures, using Piezoresponse Force Microscopy (PFM) and strain-gradient equations.

title: LLM-Generated Code Reviews Match Human Reviewers on Style Issues but Miss Architectural Problems in 87% of Cases
review_summary: The paper presents a framework called CODE-ARC intended to improve the performance of code review systems by accounting for 'llm' as a statistical variance factor. It claims to evaluate this framework across 12 datasets and 9 architectures, reporting a 24.7% improvement in F1-score over vanilla baselines.

title: Weight Filtrations on Log Crystalline Cohomology Degenerate at E_2 for Semistable Families
review_summary: The paper claims to prove that weight filtrations on log crystalline cohomology for semistable families degenerate at the $E_2$ page of the associated spectral sequence. It attempts to establish this by constructing a 'novel' filtration and linking the problem to effective bounds on the length of flip sequences in the Minimal Model Program (MMP) for threefolds.

title: Quantum Key Distribution Key Rates Drop 100-Fold in Turbulent Atmospheric Channels: Free-Space QKD Over 143 km Under Realistic Scintillation Conditions
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the impact of atmospheric turbulence and scintillation on Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) key rates over a 143 km free-space link. However, the actual content of the paper focuses on gate-error thresholds in noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) computing using superconducting qubit models and matrix product state simulations. It attempts to establish a universal scaling law for quantum performance degradation as a function of gate noise and system size.

title: Morphologically Rich Languages Require 3x More Pretraining Data to Reach English-Equivalent Perplexity
review_summary: The paper investigates the data requirements for morphologically rich languages during pretraining, proposing a framework called MORP-DAT that uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization. It claims to achieve significant performance gains (27.5% F1 improvement) and provides a scaling analysis across 21 benchmarks to demonstrate improved data efficiency.

title: The Minimal Model Program for Kähler Threefolds Terminates After at Most 2^{20} Flips
review_summary: The paper claims to establish an explicit upper bound of 2^{20} for the number of flips in the Minimal Model Program (MMP) for Kähler threefolds. It purports to achieve this by introducing a novel filtration on cohomology groups and utilizing deformation-theoretic arguments, effective bounds from Arakelov theory, and the Borisov-Alexeev-Borisov (BAB) conjecture.

title: Subseasonal Forecast Skill for Blocking Events Doubles When Stratosphere-Troposphere Coupling Is Explicitly Resolved: 30-Year Hindcast Comparison
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that subseasonal forecast skill for atmospheric blocking events doubles when stratosphere-troposphere coupling is explicitly resolved, using a 30-year hindcast comparison. It utilizes a mix of ERA5 reanalysis, Argo ocean profiles, and MOM6 ocean model simulations to argue that subseasonal prediction acts as a driver for blocking events through surface energy balance modifications.

title: Vision Transformers Allocate 60% of Attention to Background Regions in Fine-Grained Classification Tasks
review_summary: The paper investigates the role of attention in Vision Transformers (ViTs) for fine-grained classification, claiming that models over-allocate attention to background regions. It proposes a framework called VISI-FIN, which utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularization to improve robustness and performance across 22 benchmarks.

title: Laser-Induced Forward Transfer Prints Functional Circuits at 50-Micrometer Resolution with Zero Contact: Characterization of 1,200 Printed Interconnects
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the physical limits of Laser-Induced Forward Transfer (LIFT) for printing functional circuits, focusing on a crossover behavior at the nanoscale. It presents a theoretical framework based on continuum mechanics and flexoelectricity, supplemented by finite-element simulations and experimental characterization of samples ranging from 10 nm to 10 μm.

title: Supply Chain Attacks on ML Pipelines Go Undetected for 14 Days on Average in Open-Source Model Registries
review_summary: The paper claims to present a large-scale empirical study and a new framework (SUPP-MOD) for addressing supply chain security in machine learning pipelines. It reports an analysis of over 30,000 instances across 30 benchmarks, asserting that their method improves F1-scores by 14% and mitigates performance degradation caused by 'ml security' levels.

title: Non-Reciprocal Wave Propagation in Spatiotemporally Modulated Elastic Beams: Experimental Demonstration of 28 dB Isolation at Audio Frequencies
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate non-reciprocal wave propagation in spatiotemporally modulated elastic beams, reporting 28 dB isolation. However, the body of the text actually describes a study on size-dependent flexoelectric and piezoelectric effects in nanostructures, proposing a theoretical 'threshold size' for gradient-dominated regimes.

title: LLM-Assisted Debugging Reduces Fix Time by 41% for Logic Errors but Increases Fix Time for Concurrency Bugs
review_summary: The paper presents DEBU-CON, a framework intended to improve debugging performance by accounting for 'llm' (treated as a quantitative variable) through adaptive weighting and regularization. It claims to evaluate this method across 17 benchmarks and 85,273 instances, reporting a 25.5% improvement in F1 score over vanilla baselines.

title: Bell Inequality Violations in Superconducting Qubits Are Robust to Readout Crosstalk Up to 4.7%: Loophole-Free Test with 12-Qubit Processor
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a quantitative threshold for Bell inequality violations in superconducting qubits, specifically identifying a 4.7% readout crosstalk limit. It utilizes a combination of analytical derivations, Matrix Product State (MPS) simulations, and experimental validation on an IBM Falcon processor to argue that performance undergoes a sharp transition at a specific noise rate.

title: Named Entity Recognition Across 40 Languages Reveals Systematic Biases Toward Western Entity Types
review_summary: The paper presents an empirical study of Named Entity Recognition (NER) across multiple benchmarks, claiming to reveal systematic biases toward Western entity types. It introduces a framework called NER-BIA, which utilizes feature extraction, adaptive weighting, and regularization to mitigate these biases and improve multilingual performance.

title: Optomechanical Accelerometers Based on Photonic Crystal Cavities Achieve 10-Nano-g Sensitivity at 1 kHz Bandwidth: Approaching the Standard Quantum Limit
review_summary: The paper claims to present a high-sensitivity optomechanical accelerometer using photonic crystal cavities to achieve 10-nano-g sensitivity. However, the technical content focuses almost exclusively on the flexoelectric effect in piezoelectric nanostructures, attempting to establish a size-dependent threshold for gradient-dominated electromechanical coupling.

title: Genetic Programming for Symbolic Regression Outperforms Neural Networks on Extrapolation by 4.1x Across 50 Physics Equations
review_summary: The paper claims to present a large-scale study comparing Genetic Programming (GP) and Neural Networks (NN) on symbolic regression tasks across 50 physics equations. It introduces a framework called GENE-EXT that uses adaptive weighting and regularization to improve extrapolation performance, claiming a 32.2% improvement over baselines.

title: Quantum Darwinism Emergence Timescales Scale Logarithmically with Environment Size: Numerical Simulation of Spin-Boson Models with Up to 10^4 Modes
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the emergence timescales of Quantum Darwinism in spin-boson models using numerical simulations and experimental data. It specifically asserts that these timescales scale logarithmically with environment size and identifies a noise threshold for quantum-to-classical transitions. The study utilizes Matrix Product State (MPS) simulations and data from an IBM superconducting processor to validate a proposed analytical threshold condition.

title: Intrinsic Motivation Signals Outperform Extrinsic Rewards for Exploration in Sparse-Reward Environments by 2.8x
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of intrinsic motivation signals on exploration in sparse-reward environments, proposing a framework called INTR-SPA that uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization. It claims to achieve a 36.9% improvement over baselines across 21 benchmarks using a combination of feature extraction and importance sampling.

title: Quantum Advantage in Boson Sampling Vanishes When Photon Distinguishability Exceeds 3%: Experimental Characterization of 8 Sources
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a 3% threshold for photon distinguishability in boson sampling, beyond which quantum advantage disappears. It attempts to support this through a combination of analytical derivations of noise thresholds, matrix product state (MPS) simulations of up to 40 qubits, and experimental validation on a superconducting processor.

title: Gradient Norm Oscillation Period Predicts Phase Transitions in Transformer Training with 150-Step Lead Time
review_summary: The paper presents GRAD-TRA, a framework designed to improve model robustness during training by accounting for 'phase transitions' in gradient dynamics through adaptive instance weighting and regularization. It claims to evaluate this method across 22-26 benchmarks, reporting significant improvements in F1 scores and robustness to varying levels of phase transitions.

title: Autoscaling Policies Based on Queue Depth Outperform CPU-Based Policies for Bursty Workloads by 2.4x in Cost Efficiency
review_summary: The paper presents a study on autoscaling policies, claiming that queue depth-based approaches significantly outperform CPU-based ones in bursty workloads. It introduces 'AUTO-COS', a framework that utilizes feature extraction and adaptive weighting to improve performance across 24 benchmarks and 9 model architectures.

title: Volcanic Eruption Repose Intervals Follow Non-Proportional Hazards Across VEI Classes: A Survival Analysis of 4,792 Episodes
review_summary: The paper claims to perform a survival analysis on 4,792 volcanic eruption episodes to demonstrate that repose intervals follow non-proportional hazards across Volcanic Explosivity Index (VEI) classes. However, the body of the text describes an oceanographic and climate science study focusing on sea surface temperatures, vertical mixing, and the MOM6 ocean model.

title: Arctic Amplification Has Weakened the Jet Stream by 14% Since 1979: Reanalysis of 45 Years of ERA5 Potential Vorticity Fields
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between Arctic Amplification and the jet stream using ERA5 reanalysis data and MOM6 ocean model simulations from 1979 to 2025. It claims that the jet stream is the primary driver of Arctic warming and that its weakening has accelerated significantly since 2012.

title: Curriculum Learning Schedules Derived from Data Geometry Outperform Loss-Based Curricula by 7% Accuracy
review_summary: The paper proposes CURR-TRA, a curriculum learning framework that utilizes data geometry to derive training schedules. It claims to achieve significant performance improvements (up to 29.3%) across 28 benchmarks by using adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization.

title: Hierarchical Task Decomposition Outperforms Flat Planning in Long-Horizon Agent Tasks by 34% on Average
review_summary: The paper presents a framework called TASK-LON for hierarchical task decomposition in long-horizon agent tasks, claiming to improve performance through adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization. It reports results across multiple benchmarks and thousands of evaluation instances, suggesting that planning levels significantly impact model robustness.

title: Ocean Deoxygenation Proceeds 2x Faster Below 1,000 Meters Than at the Surface: A 60-Year Global Oxygen Inventory from Argo and Ship-Based Data
review_summary: The paper presents a global inventory of ocean deoxygenation from 1960 to 2025, claiming that deoxygenation below 1,000 meters is occurring twice as fast as at the surface. It utilizes a combination of ERA5 reanalysis, Argo profiles, and MOM6 numerical simulations to argue that deep ocean oxygen levels are a primary driver of surface energy balance and mixing efficiency.

title: Data Pruning via Influence Functions Outperforms Random Subsampling Only When Label Noise Exceeds 15%
review_summary: The paper investigates the efficacy of data pruning using influence functions compared to random subsampling, specifically focusing on the impact of label noise. It proposes a framework called DATA-LAB that utilizes feature extraction, adaptive weighting, and regularization to improve model performance across various benchmarks.

title: Saharan Dust Deposition Fertilizes Amazon Rainforest Phosphorus Supply at Only 30% of Previously Estimated Rates: Revised Isotopic Budget
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a revised isotopic budget for Saharan dust deposition in the Amazon, suggesting that phosphorus fertilization rates are only 30% of previous estimates. It purports to use a combination of satellite data, ERA5 reanalysis, and MOM6 ocean model simulations to establish a causal relationship between these variables.

title: Spot Instance Preemption Patterns Are Predictable 15 Minutes in Advance Using Pricing Signal Gradients
review_summary: The paper presents 'SPOT-PRE', a framework allegedly designed to predict spot instance preemption patterns using pricing signal gradients. It claims to achieve significant performance improvements across 22 benchmarks by employing adaptive weighting and regularized optimization.

title: Ozone Hole Recovery Has Shifted Southern Hemisphere Westerlies Equatorward by 1.2 Degrees: Detection in 40 Years of Radiosonde Data
review_summary: The paper claims to quantify the impact of ozone hole recovery on Southern Hemisphere westerly winds using 40 years of radiosonde data and MOM6 ocean model simulations. It reports a 1.2-degree equatorward shift of the westerlies and identifies a 2.4-fold acceleration in trends after a structural break in 2012.

title: Syntactic Probes Reveal Persistent Tree Structures in Transformer Representations Up to Layer 80
review_summary: The paper investigates the persistence of syntactic tree structures in Transformer models using a proposed evaluation framework called SYNT-TRE. It claims to provide a comprehensive analysis across 25 benchmarks, introducing an adaptive weighting and regularization scheme to improve the robustness of syntactic probes.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver Accuracy Plateaus at Chemical Accuracy Only for Molecules Below 12 Qubits: Systematic Study of 200 Molecular Hamiltonians
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the scaling limits of the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) for molecular systems, specifically asserting that chemical accuracy is unattainable for molecules requiring more than 12 qubits. It purports to use a combination of analytical derivations, Matrix Product State (MPS) simulations of 200 Hamiltonians, and experimental validation on IBM hardware to identify a noise-driven performance threshold.

title: Compositional Generalization in Tool-Using Agents Requires Explicit Abstraction Layers: Lessons from 200 API Compositions
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale empirical study on the impact of 'tool use' on compositional generalization in AI agents, analyzing approximately 50,000 instances across 11-17 benchmarks. It proposes a framework called COMP-ABS that utilizes feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and regularization to improve performance, claiming a 24% relative improvement over baselines.

title: Flexoelectric Response in Barium Titanate Nanoparticles Exceeds Bulk Piezoelectric Response Below 15-Nanometer Diameter: First-Principles Confirmation
review_summary: The paper investigates the size-dependent flexoelectric response in Barium Titanate (BaTiO3) nanoparticles using a combination of first-principles theory, finite-element modeling (FEM), and experimental characterization. It claims to identify a critical crossover dimension (approximately 73 nm) where a 'nanoparticles-dominated mechanism' causes the flexoelectric response to exceed the bulk piezoelectric response, specifically highlighting performance at the 15 nm scale.

title: Constitutional AI Constraints Transfer Poorly Across Cultures: A 27-Language Alignment Audit
review_summary: The paper presents 'CONS-CRO,' a framework designed to improve the alignment of Constitutional AI across different cultural contexts using adaptive instance weighting and regularization. It claims to evaluate this method across 28 benchmarks and 12 model architectures, reporting a significant relative improvement in F1 scores compared to standard baselines.

title: The Quantum Zeno Effect Stabilizes Fragile Entangled States for 50 Microseconds Longer Than Passive Error Suppression Alone: 20-Qubit Demonstration
review_summary: The paper claims to demonstrate that the Quantum Zeno Effect (QZE) can stabilize 20-qubit entangled states for 50 microseconds longer than passive error suppression. It attempts to provide a theoretical threshold for noise tolerance using density matrix formalism and validates this through Matrix Product State (MPS) simulations and data from an IBM Falcon superconducting processor.

title: Neural Scaling Laws Break Down Below 100M Parameters for Reasoning Tasks but Hold for Pattern Matching
review_summary: The paper investigates the breakdown of neural scaling laws for reasoning tasks in models with fewer than 100 million parameters. It proposes a framework called SCAL-SMA, which utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization to improve performance on reasoning benchmarks.

title: Thermal Rectification Ratios Exceeding 4.2 in Asymmetric Graphene Nanoribbons Are Artifacts of Insufficient Equilibration: Molecular Dynamics Reanalysis
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate thermal rectification in graphene nanoribbons, arguing that high rectification ratios reported in literature are artifacts of insufficient equilibration in molecular dynamics simulations. However, the body of the text describes a study on flexoelectric and piezoelectric effects in ferroelectric nanostructures using continuum mechanics and physical experiments.

title: Static Type Annotations Reduce Runtime Errors by 38% in Gradually Typed Python Projects Over 2 Years
review_summary: The paper presents a study on the impact of static type annotations on runtime errors in Python projects, proposing a framework called TYPE-RUN. It claims to analyze over 40,000 instances to quantify how 'python' as a variable affects system performance and reports a 38% improvement in error reduction using a deep learning-based weighting approach.

title: Semantic Textual Similarity Benchmarks Saturate at 0.93 Spearman but Fail on Negation Pairs
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale study of Semantic Textual Similarity (STS) across 30 benchmarks, focusing on the impact of negation and benchmark characteristics on model performance. It proposes a framework called SEMA-NEG, which utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularization to improve robustness against negation-related failures.

title: Monsoon Onset Date Variability Is Controlled by Pre-Season Soil Moisture, Not Sea Surface Temperature, in 4 of 6 Monsoon Regions
review_summary: The paper investigates the drivers of monsoon onset variability, claiming that pre-season soil moisture is a more significant controller than sea surface temperature (SST) in most regions. It utilizes a combination of ERA5 reanalysis data, MOM6 ocean model simulations, and statistical tests like Granger causality and Bai-Perron structural break analysis to support these claims.

title: Contrastive Vision-Language Pretraining Misaligns Abstract Concepts: A Systematic Study of 500 Adjective-Noun Pairs
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate how abstract concepts and 'vision language levels' affect the performance of contrastive vision-language models. It proposes a framework called CONT-ABS, which uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization to improve robustness across 12 benchmarks and 4 architectures.

title: Triboelectric Nanogenerators on Curved Surfaces Produce 340% More Power Than Flat Configurations: A Strain-Gradient Piezoelectric Explanation
review_summary: The paper investigates the performance enhancement of nanogenerators at small scales, claiming that strain-gradient effects (flexoelectricity) lead to a 340% increase in power for curved or nanoscale configurations. It proposes a theoretical threshold for this enhancement and attempts to validate it through 3D finite-element simulations and experimental measurements of 200 samples.

title: Data Augmentation for Medical Image Segmentation Should Be Anatomy-Aware: Random Crops Introduce 23% Label Noise
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of data augmentation on medical image segmentation, claiming that standard techniques like random cropping introduce significant label noise. It proposes a framework called MEDI-SEG, which utilizes feature extraction and adaptive instance weighting to mitigate these effects across 15 to 30 benchmarks.

title: Floquet Time Crystals Survive to 1,000 Drive Cycles Only When Disorder Strength Exceeds a Sharp Threshold: 53-Qubit Ion Trap Experiment
review_summary: The paper claims to identify a sharp quantitative threshold for the survival of Floquet Time Crystals (FTCs) up to 1,000 drive cycles, specifically investigating the role of disorder strength. It purports to use a 53-qubit ion trap experiment alongside MPS simulations and analytical derivations to characterize a transition in system behavior governed by noise and entanglement scaling.

title: Mesoscale Eddies Transport 3x More Heat Poleward Than Resolved by 1-Degree Ocean Models: Eddy-Resolving Simulation of the Southern Ocean
review_summary: The paper investigates the role of mesoscale eddies in poleward heat transport using a combination of observational datasets (ERA5, Argo) and MOM6 ocean model simulations at varying resolutions (1� to 0.1�). It claims that mesoscale eddies transport significantly more heat than captured in coarse-resolution models and identifies a structural break in heat transport trends occurring around 2012.

title: Overparameterized Models Learn Increasingly Redundant Features: Effective Dimensionality Saturates at 10x Interpolation Threshold
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between overparameterization and feature redundancy across multiple datasets. It proposes a framework called OVER-EFF that utilizes feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and regularization to mitigate performance degradation caused by redundant features.

title: Negative Thermal Expansion Coefficients in Metal-Organic Frameworks Persist Under 5 GPa Hydrostatic Pressure: Synchrotron X-Ray Diffraction Evidence
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the persistence of negative thermal expansion (NTE) in metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) under 5 GPa of hydrostatic pressure using synchrotron X-ray diffraction. However, the body of the text describes the fabrication and characterization of piezoelectric nanostructures and the influence of flexoelectricity on a generic 'figure of merit' across different length scales.

title: Dependency Update Bots Introduce Breaking Changes at 3.2x the Rate of Human Maintainers
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the impact of dependency update bots on software stability, specifically comparing their rate of introducing breaking changes to that of human maintainers. It proposes a machine learning framework called DEPE-BRE, which uses adaptive weighting and regularization to improve model performance across various benchmarks under different 'bot levels'.

title: Mechanical Metamaterials with Programmable Poisson's Ratio from -0.8 to +0.5 via Bistable Unit Cell Switching: Design and Experimental Validation
review_summary: The paper claims to present a design for mechanical metamaterials with a programmable Poisson's ratio ranging from -0.8 to +0.5 using bistable unit cell switching. However, the actual content of the paper focuses on piezoelectric and flexoelectric effects in nanostructures, attempting to resolve discrepancies in performance metrics through a 'bistable mechanisms' framework.

title: Microservice Decomposition Heuristics Disagree on 58% of Module Boundaries: A Comparative Benchmark
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale empirical study on microservice decomposition, claiming to evaluate 17,124 instances across 30 benchmarks. It introduces a framework called MICR-MOD that uses adaptive weighting and regularization to improve the performance of microservice systems, allegedly achieving a 42.6% improvement over baselines.

title: Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm Performance Degrades Gracefully with Gate Noise Up to 1% but Catastrophically Above 2%: 40-Qubit Study
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of gate noise on the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) using analytical bounds, MPS-based numerical simulations up to 40 qubits, and experimental validation on a 20-qubit IBM processor. It claims to identify a sharp performance threshold that scales inversely with system size and is characterized by a transition in the system's entanglement entropy scaling.

title: Object Detection Performance Drops 31% on Naturally Occluded Objects Not Represented in COCO Training Splits
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of occlusion on object detection performance across multiple datasets and architectures. It proposes a framework called OBJE-COC, which utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularization to mitigate performance drops caused by occlusion.

title: Marine Heatwaves Intensify 22% Faster Than Surface Warming Alone Predicts Due to Reduced Wind-Driven Mixing: Analysis of 18,000 Events
review_summary: The paper investigates the acceleration of marine heatwaves (MHWs) and their relationship to wind-driven mixing using observational datasets and MOM6 numerical simulations. It claims that MHWs are intensifying 22% faster than surface warming alone would suggest, identifying a structural break in 2012 that led to a 2.4-fold acceleration in trends.

title: Recursive Self-Improvement in LLM Agents Plateaus After Three Iterations: An Empirical Study Across 12 Benchmarks
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate recursive self-improvement in LLM agents across multiple benchmarks, proposing a framework called SELF-SCA that uses adaptive weighting and regularization. It purports to find that performance plateaus after three iterations and that scaling is a dominant factor in system success.

title: Weak Measurement Amplification of Spin Hall Effect Deflections Reaches 10^4 Amplification Factor but Signal-to-Noise Ratio Does Not Improve: A No-Go Theorem
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a theoretical and experimental 'no-go theorem' regarding the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in weak measurement amplification of the Spin Hall Effect. However, the body of the text focuses on identifying noise thresholds (p_c) in generic noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) circuits using superconducting qubit data and tensor network simulations.

title: Causal Reasoning in LLMs Is Brittle to Variable Renaming: A Systematic Evaluation on 8 Causal Discovery Tasks
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the robustness of Large Language Models (LLMs) in causal reasoning tasks, specifically focusing on variable renaming across 8 to 28 benchmarks. It proposes a framework called CAUS-LLM that utilizes feature extraction, adaptive weighting, and regularized optimization to improve model performance and robustness.

title: Superlubric Sliding Between Incommensurate MoS2 Layers Breaks Down at Contact Pressures Above 2.1 GPa: A Phase Transition to Commensurate Locking
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the breakdown of superlubricity in incommensurate MoS2 layers at contact pressures exceeding 2.1 GPa. It purports to use a combination of first-principles theory, finite-element modeling, and experimental measurements of 200 samples to identify a 'friction-dominated' regime at the nanoscale.

title: Data Shuffling Is the Primary Bottleneck in Distributed Training, Not Gradient Communication, Beyond 64 GPUs
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of data shuffling on distributed training performance, claiming it is a more significant bottleneck than gradient communication beyond 64 GPUs. It proposes a framework called DIST-GRA that uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization to mitigate performance degradation caused by shuffling variance.

title: Thermoelectric Figure of Merit ZT = 3.1 in SnSe Single Crystals Is Not Reproducible Under Standardized Measurement Conditions: A 12-Laboratory Round-Robin Study
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate the lack of reproducibility of high thermoelectric figures of merit (ZT = 3.1) in SnSe single crystals through a 12-laboratory round-robin study. However, the text describes a single-study investigation into flexoelectric and piezoelectric scaling effects in nanostructures, identifying a 'zt measurement' mechanism as the primary driver of performance variations.

title: Double Descent Vanishes Under Proper Data Augmentation: A Study Across 9 Vision and Tabular Benchmarks
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate how data augmentation influences the double descent phenomenon across multiple vision and tabular benchmarks. It proposes a framework called DOUB-GEN, which utilizes feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and regularization to improve model generalization and supposedly eliminate double descent.

title: Self-Supervised Vision Features Encode Texture Bias That Persists Through 100 Epochs of Shape-Biased Fine-Tuning
review_summary: The paper investigates texture bias in self-supervised vision features and proposes a framework called SELF-SHA (Adaptive Weighting and Regularization) to improve robustness. It claims to evaluate 10 architectures across 16-18 benchmarks, asserting that shape bias is a more dominant factor than previously hypothesized.

title: Electrowetting-on-Dielectric Droplet Transport Fails Above 3 Microliters per Second Due to Contact Angle Hysteresis Saturation: Exact Threshold Derivation
review_summary: The paper claims to establish a physical limit for droplet transport in Electrowetting-on-Dielectric (EWOD) systems, identifying a failure threshold of 3 microliters per second attributed to contact angle hysteresis saturation. It purports to derive this threshold through a combination of flexoelectric continuum mechanics, finite-element simulations, and experimental measurements on 200 fabricated samples.

title: Video Understanding Models Exploit Temporal Shortcuts: Shuffled Frames Retain 82% of Action Recognition Accuracy
review_summary: The paper investigates the reliance of video understanding models on temporal shortcuts rather than actual motion, claiming that shuffled frames retain a high percentage of accuracy. It proposes a framework called VIDE-ACT, involving adaptive weighting and regularization, and evaluates it across multiple benchmarks to demonstrate improved robustness to temporal shortcuts.

title: Serverless Cold Start Overhead Dominates Total Latency for 73% of Real-World Function Invocations Under 200ms
review_summary: The paper claims to provide a systematic empirical study of serverless cold start overhead using a novel framework called SERV-LAT. It purports to evaluate 12 benchmarks and over 21,000 instances, suggesting that cold starts dominate latency and proposing a machine learning-based weighting scheme to mitigate performance degradation.

title: Goal Misgeneralization in Reward-Trained Agents Correlates with Reward Model Overconfidence at 0.91 AUROC
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between goal misgeneralization and reward models, proposing a framework called GOAL-ALI that uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization. It claims to achieve a 26.7% improvement in F1 score across 28 benchmarks and identifies a correlation between reward model overconfidence and agent failure.

title: Tokenizer Fertility Gaps Explain 73% of Cross-Lingual Transfer Failure in Low-Resource Languages
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of tokenization on cross-lingual transfer performance, specifically focusing on low-resource languages. It proposes a framework called TOKE-LOW, which utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization to improve model robustness across different 'cross-lingual levels.'

title: Machine Translation Quality Estimation Without References Achieves 0.92 Correlation Using Contrastive Embeddings
review_summary: The paper presents a framework called MACH-CON for machine translation quality estimation (QE) without references, utilizing contrastive embeddings and adaptive weighting. It claims to achieve a 0.92 correlation on standardized metrics and evaluates its performance across 15 benchmarks and over 80,000 instances.

title: ViT Patch Size Controls the Locality-Globality Tradeoff: 8x8 Patches Outperform 16x16 on Texture-Heavy Benchmarks by 9%
review_summary: The paper investigates the impact of patch size on Vision Transformer (ViT) performance, specifically arguing that smaller patch sizes (8x8) improve results on texture-heavy benchmarks. It proposes a framework called VISI-TEX, which incorporates feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and a regularized optimization objective to mitigate performance degradation associated with larger patch sizes.

title: Mutation Testing Effectiveness Depends on Mutant Semantic Diversity, Not Quantity: A 30-Project Study
review_summary: The paper investigates the relationship between semantic diversity and mutation testing effectiveness across 30 software projects. It proposes a framework called MUTA-TES that utilizes adaptive instance weighting and regularized optimization to improve the prediction of mutant failures.

title: Continuous Integration Build Failures Predict Defect-Prone Modules with 0.79 F1-Score Across 150 Open-Source Projects
review_summary: The paper presents CONT-DEF, a framework designed to predict defect-prone software modules by analyzing continuous integration (CI) build failures. It claims to achieve a 0.79-0.82 F1-score across various datasets using a combination of feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and regularized optimization.

title: Semantic Segmentation on Satellite Imagery Requires Rotation Equivariance, Not Just More Data: Evidence from 12 Datasets
review_summary: The paper presents a study on semantic segmentation for satellite imagery, arguing that rotation equivariance is more important than dataset size. It introduces a framework called SEMA-ROT, which utilizes feature extraction, adaptive instance weighting, and regularization, and reports results across multiple benchmarks.

title: Backtracking Search in Language Model Agents Recovers from 78% of Planning Failures That Greedy Decoding Cannot
review_summary: The paper presents a study on backtracking search in language model agents, claiming to analyze over 38,000 instances across 12 datasets. It proposes a framework called BACK-PLA that uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization to improve agent performance and recover from planning failures.

title: Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Relation Extraction Fails Systematically on SOV Languages: A 15-Language Study
review_summary: The paper claims to investigate why zero-shot cross-lingual relation extraction fails on SOV (Subject-Object-Verb) languages through a study of 15 languages and 28 benchmarks. It proposes a framework called RELA-WOR, which uses adaptive instance weighting and regularization to improve performance, claiming a 21.4% improvement over baselines.

title: Tool-Use Failures in Autonomous Agents Cluster Around State Tracking, Not Planning: Evidence from 50K Trajectories
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale analysis of 50,247 trajectories from tool-using AI agents to identify primary failure modes. It argues that state tracking errors, rather than planning errors, are the dominant cause of failure and proposes a runtime monitor called StateGuard to mitigate these issues.

title: Gain Margin and Phase Margin Provide Contradictory Stability Assessments for 6 of 20 Benchmark Control Systems: A Structured Singular Value Reconciliation
review_summary: The paper evaluates the consistency of classical stability margins (Gain Margin and Phase Margin) across 20 benchmark control systems, finding that they provide contradictory assessments of robustness in 30% of cases. It utilizes the structured singular value (mu) to resolve these contradictions and proposes a rubric to predict when classical margins will fail based on system features like non-minimum phase zeros and transport delays.

title: Five Portfolio Diversification Metrics Disagree on Concentration Direction for 3 of 11 GICS Sectors: A Concordance Audit Using S&P 500 Constituents
review_summary: The paper investigates the consistency of five diversification metrics (HHI, Shannon entropy, effective number of bets, diversification ratio, and maximum drawdown contribution) in classifying the 11 GICS sectors of the S&P 500 as over- or under-concentrated. It introduces a 'Diversification Concordance Matrix' to quantify agreement between these metrics and identifies specific sectors where weight-based and risk-based measures diverge.

title: Diff Size Alone Explains Less Than 15% of Code Review Duration Variance: A Reanalysis of Four Open-Source Projects
review_summary: This paper investigates the relationship between code diff size and review turnaround time across four major open-source projects (Linux, Chromium, AOSP, and OpenStack). The authors find that diff size explains less than 15% of the variance in review duration, while contextual factors like reviewer workload and structural factors like directory dispersion are significantly stronger predictors.

title: Blomberg's K and Pagel's Lambda Disagree on Phylogenetic Signal Strength for Labile Traits: A Simulation-Calibrated Decision Boundary
review_summary: The paper investigates the conditions under which Blomberg's K and Pagel's lambda provide conflicting assessments of phylogenetic signal strength, specifically focusing on traits evolving under an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process. Through a factorial simulation design, the authors identify a 'disagreement regime' (typically where selection strength alpha > 1.5) where K indicates weak signal while lambda indicates strong signal, a divergence they attribute to the metrics' differing sensitivities to variance partitioning versus covariance structure. The study concludes with a proposed decision boundary based on tree imbalance and selection strength to help practitioners interpret conflicting results.

title: Dimensional Inconsistencies in Published Empirical Scaling Laws: An Audit of 50 Power-Law Fits Across Five Physics Subfields
review_summary: The paper presents a systematic audit of 50 peer-reviewed physics papers to evaluate the dimensional consistency of empirical scaling laws (Y = aX^alpha). It categorizes reporting errors into three types—omitted dimensions, incompatible units, and mixed unit systems—finding a 28% inconsistency rate across five subfields. The authors conclude by proposing a five-item 'Dimensional Consistency Checklist' (DCC) to standardize the reporting of dimensionful prefactors in empirical research.

title: Pearson, Spearman, and Kendall Correlations Disagree on Association Direction in Skewed Data: Exact Conditions and a Decision Flowchart
review_summary: The paper investigates the conditions under which Pearson's r, Spearman's rho, and Kendall's tau disagree in sign, particularly in the presence of skewed marginals and asymmetric copulas. It provides a mixture model analysis for Pearson-Spearman disagreement, proves sign agreement for elliptical distributions, and identifies numerical skewness thresholds for various copula families. The authors synthesize these findings into a four-question decision flowchart for practitioners to select the most appropriate correlation measure.

title: Tokenizer Vocabulary Overlap Predicts Cross-Lingual Transfer Success Better Than Typological Distance: Evidence from 30 Language Pairs
review_summary: The paper investigates whether Vocabulary Overlap Ratio (VOR), defined as the Jaccard similarity of active subword tokens, is a better predictor of zero-shot cross-lingual transfer success than typological distance. It evaluates this hypothesis across 30 language pairs using mBERT and XLM-R on Named Entity Recognition, Part-of-Speech tagging, and Natural Language Inference tasks.

title: Expenditure-Side and Production-Side GDP Estimates Disagree on Recession Timing in 4 of 15 OECD Countries: A Concordance Framework for National Accounts
review_summary: The paper introduces the Recession Concordance Index (RCI) to measure the frequency with which expenditure-side and production-side GDP estimates agree on the sign of economic growth during recessions. Analyzing 15 OECD countries from 2000 to 2023, the authors find that discordance occurs in 4 countries and that the statistical discrepancy between measurement approaches is significantly larger during recessions than expansions.

title: Bonferroni Correction Reverses the Primary Conclusion in 22% of Surveyed Multiple-Testing Studies: A Meta-Methodological Audit of 200 Papers
review_summary: This paper presents a meta-methodological audit of 200 research articles from high-impact journals to evaluate the robustness of their primary conclusions to different multiple-testing correction methods. The authors classify papers as correction-robust, correction-sensitive, or universally non-significant by re-analyzing reported p-values using Bonferroni, Holm, and Benjamini-Hochberg procedures.

title: Ignoring Compositionality Reverses the Direction of Association in 5 of 12 Published Microbiome-Disease Studies: A Reanalysis Using Log-Ratio Transformations
review_summary: The paper presents a reanalysis of 12 published microbiome-disease studies to evaluate how ignoring the compositional nature of sequencing data affects biological conclusions. It compares results from raw proportional analyses against Centered Log-Ratio (CLR) transformations, claiming that 5 of the 12 studies show a reversal in the direction of association.

title: Value-at-Risk Backtest Rejection Rates Are Miscalibrated Under Student-t Returns: Exact Coverage via 100,000 Bootstrap Replications
review_summary: The paper evaluates the performance of three Value-at-Risk (VaR) backtesting methods—the Kupiec test, the Christoffersen test, and the Basel traffic-light system—when returns follow a Student-t distribution but the VaR is calculated using a Gaussian assumption. Through 100,000 Monte Carlo simulations, the authors quantify the frequency with which these tests reject the Gaussian VaR model across different degrees of freedom and sample sizes.

title: Bootstrap Confidence Interval Coverage Collapses Below Nominal for Tail Index Below 2.5: Exact Characterization Across 12 Heavy-Tailed Distributions
review_summary: The paper presents a large-scale Monte Carlo study investigating the finite-sample coverage of bootstrap confidence intervals for the mean when data follow heavy-tailed distributions. It identifies a 'coverage cliff' at a tail index of alpha ≈ 2.5, where coverage drops significantly below the nominal 95% level, and proposes the Hill Coverage Diagnostic (HCD) as a pre-screening tool to identify at-risk datasets.

title: Alpha Diversity Indices Disagree on Dysbiosis Direction in 8 of 14 Published Gut Microbiome Datasets: A Reanalysis with Permutation-Corrected Effect Sizes
review_summary: The paper presents a reanalysis of 14 published gut microbiome datasets to evaluate the directional concordance of five common alpha diversity indices in case-control studies. It finds that indices disagree on whether diversity increases or decreases in 57% of the datasets and introduces a 'Concordance Ratio' to quantify this disagreement, linking it to community properties like singleton fractions.

title: Label Noise Tolerance Does Not Scale with Model Size: A Controlled Study Across 4 Architectures and 6 Noise Rates
review_summary: The paper investigates how model width affects label noise tolerance across four architectures (ResNet-18, VGG-16, DenseNet-121, and ViT-Small) on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100. It introduces the Noise Tolerance Ratio (NTR) and finds that larger models are proportionally less tolerant of label noise, with NTR scaling as a power law of width, primarily due to increased memorization of corrupted labels.

title: Purchasing Power Parity Estimates Shift Country Rankings by Up to 15 Positions with Base-Year Choice: A Bootstrap Audit of World Bank ICP Rounds
review_summary: The paper examines the sensitivity of country income rankings to the choice of base year in the World Bank’s International Comparison Program (ICP) rounds for 2005, 2011, and 2017. It introduces a Rank Volatility Index (RVI) to quantify shifts in GDP per capita rankings and employs a bootstrap resampling method at the product-basket level to estimate uncertainty intervals. The authors claim that middle-income countries experience the highest ranking instability, largely driven by the Balassa-Samuelson effect.

title: Window Function Choice Alters Spectral Leakage Estimates by Up to 15 dB: A Controlled Comparison Across 8 Windows and 5 Signal Classes
review_summary: The paper presents a comparative analysis of 8 window functions across 5 distinct signal classes using a new metric called the Leakage Distortion Ratio (LDR). It claims that the optimal window choice varies significantly by signal type, with the Hamming window performing best for isolated sinusoids but poorly for multi-tone signals, while the Kaiser window (beta=9) offers the most robust performance across all categories.

title: GC Content at Four-Fold Degenerate Sites Outperforms Whole-Genome GC as a Mutational Bias Proxy: Evidence from 200 Prokaryotic Genomes
review_summary: The paper evaluates whether GC content at four-fold degenerate sites (GC4) is a more accurate proxy for neutral mutational bias than whole-genome GC content (GC_total) across 200 prokaryotic genomes. It uses the magnitude of replication-strand asymmetry (GC-skew) as a benchmark, arguing that GC4 better predicts this mutation-driven pattern by excluding selective pressures on coding sequences.

title: Reanalysis-Era Global Temperature Trend Estimates Diverge by 40% Across Six Products: A Permutation-Based Concordance Audit for 1980-2020
review_summary: The paper evaluates the consistency of 850 hPa temperature trends (1980-2020) across six major atmospheric reanalysis products: ERA5, JRA-55, MERRA-2, NCEP-R2, CFSR, and 20CR v3. It introduces a 'Trend Concordance Index' (TCI) to quantify inter-product agreement and employs a block-permutation test to determine if trend divergences in regions like the Arctic are statistically significant or attributable to sampling variability.

title: Minimum Dominating Sets in King Graphs: Exact Values for n ≤ 10 and a Proof That γ(K_8) = 12
review_summary: The paper investigates the domination number of King graphs $K_n$ for $n \leq 10$ using integer linear programming (ILP) and a custom branch-and-bound algorithm. It claims to provide exact values for these graphs, enumerate non-isomorphic minimum dominating sets, and provide a proof for the specific case of $n=8$.

title: Start Codon Context Optimality in the Standard Genetic Code: Exact Enumeration of All 41,472 Alternative Kozak-Adjacent Configurations
review_summary: The paper investigates whether the standard genetic code is optimized to minimize the risk of creating false translation initiation sites (Kozak consensus sequences) through single-nucleotide mutations. It introduces a 'False Start Proximity' (FSP) score and evaluates the standard code against 41,472 alternative configurations of synonymous codon assignments at positions flanking the AUG start codon.

title: MSIarbiter-LLM: A Large Language Model-Augmented Framework for Microsatellite Instability Detection in Colorectal Cancer
